body:
| | | | |
---
| | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWpIaeLxKjkByW4m8tv_7AlxAjpE_19c-va808s6jsr8bhnQuQ37pjxAB2eMeltXJn28zIDUYjtkWwwRs_SQfUQ7uEUNm47DWOA==&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
---
| MassGOP Monthly Update: February Edition
---
---
| Friends,
The Massachusetts Republican Party has launched 2025 with tremendous momentum! We hope you've had a great start to the new year and share our enthusiasm for the battles ahead. January was an exciting month as we welcomed a new era of American leadership with President Trump's inauguration and saw our newly elected state legislators sworn in, ready to challenge the Democratic supermajority.
Communications
This month began with the arrest of an illegal immigrant in a taxpayer-funded shelter, found with firearms and $1 million in fentanyl. House Republicans responded with a letter demanding accountability, and the [MassGOP stands with them in calling for urgent reform. ](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxULkIqmdKfydn1iM7Tk0DZWmsuvyb2Wwy6ic72cOTNMV0b5iPWx_5xsJHfJZ3MQHpBjEwwqNOnP62J-OPfQEqE2gHoc0d6gsABzabx_Mo5PHxY-kt4jtg6t7XUdy5QnjO938RwBbVku_aTLGzlcpPgoxVRQ5iOcJGei4g5nWzyjkEMX-3sg8DG5TPyg7fn5wjdhAxYlZjqVmjsMoxAE08LMnAKvgHOg6M0yLpK8JAa4eJBO0IuRiltn33UTpYPAAS&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)The state's shelter system has wasted billions while fueling a humanitarian crisis--this failure must end.
[The MassGOP's latest FOIA request](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxUI7II5gbgwnh5vAu80L0Gk7oMDucVEcqkUAt4HiTEYjY6HcfZoONsOc0V0R3PMoCIJ_BMPcFrH9942TVNSzyg0IFXQ-67kSA5jBs893Lt__5RzgA2akISsdoz9CbF_n0RxKzXKP0Gc92EkWwSmBAdgpkdeqceUmZ&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==) exposed hundreds of cases of sexual assault, physical assault, child abuse, domestic violence, and drug and human trafficking--all funded by Massachusetts taxpayers. These horrific crimes, hidden by the Healey-Driscoll Administration, occurred in migrant shelters statewide.
Governor Healey feigned outrage over the arrest but only ordered shelter inspections after the fact. Our FOIA requests prove her administration knew about these atrocities for years. [The MassGOP is holding her accountable for this full-scale, state-funded disaster.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxfTlQLWwDWiT8n1Ye2RmUBsPqEJ8UdamwnegW63d6C_cSxWg0jgAT1oHHEgTtTExeKX3rU5m8N2AH2-qO03nYffY44PIN1za7--Bv2M8TKOOyOy9IQeTh6CcoKxMqMYgcgHrbQstuA_WZOgn7PW7gcoisAZLdscHA24Zf7WtK3YtH0W0PN5Z4m8Uh65lsCtooLIrsuwBt4Iu7yn6Ce2puj4YebPLww-Ut&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
State Rep. Chris Flanagan's scandal keeps escalating. After deceiving voters with a fake alias in his last campaign, he's now under federal investigation for "alleged potential criminal activity." From campaign finance violations to reports of stolen funds, Flanagan has repeatedly shown contempt for ethics. [The MassGOP demands his immediate resignation or expulsion](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxfpWUNDQC2k8h3DALZX0xPTqVQwTgZMsuLrpPJurzKuHlDl9pMZ7gRwzhaNGkMjgbMjMTb19FGmuQXjyrQhx9wg61jmzkfMPwepKS8_tEF3VHUAt9urb1LayKc8dRpF69_jGIPf1pk_t5UjnfxSNs_On4pkr9RA0kJozGtUHY2yvFTXGYOCi2FmF4owzJZXc5u8FG0OymsANqOXlL3YboD6lcnAP-NjU1T_cs5XzgwXTJQSqp3QKpCutsVaRD-nl7OY93FOBnWxwk5VgfWOqG7A==&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==). The 1st Barnstable District deserves honest leadership.
Last month, Republicans in Congress passed the Laken Riley Act, requiring ICE to detain criminal illegal immigrants. Meanwhile, ICE arrested child predators, drug dealers, and gang members in Massachusetts--proving that sanctuary policies endanger residents. Yet, all but one member of Massachusetts' delegation voted against the act, prioritizing politics over public safety. [The MassGOP made sure voters didn't miss their betrayal.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKx-PmPMA-v0q2RrG5mDOTwqdvppotFxfk8ylg-H_ki4HAwqhNOnq8Wp7uQuTH_VYa-KXSwWP_l9s_ihLIznC4JQ5PGiXTtxAgAz0arn8jkmHPBsOoj7ZdY-9hYvCjFQmgjWTRgE3HVCiAjxW5UiSxpbAGyH-GnpK1DV7-OGxcFqkY9RPYA1ovzI9GI1UVlJHCpBNoUcvATEGM=&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
After the arrest of an illegal immigrant in a state-funded shelter, it was revealed migrants were not being screened through CORI checks--contrary to Governor Healey's claims. House Minority Leader Brad Jones condemned the administration's failure, and the MassGOP echoed his concerns. [Either Healey misled residents, or she's clueless about the crisis under her watch.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxn4kt7ag1W_C5QkInTYGsuC91w6q8lBOcsZHoXSxs879dKJIk_lhZ2NuUirlD1jtoEk02V_ljpOP_KZ09860wkKB8S78OnmrB4L0QgQtsKtZVJQGtQZiYQGBOhS9Ef_HoIRhDf9G-8BixYgap0M3MA_xLKbKuXNNNExLcMihEd21PWAB7TWHwQ9n3kJ78uns6RMZgNr_CfWR9ScrASRtQ_IgUZhhSO3qQWV2jsWly-Jonc6199P6JUYkMrcUWi2xU&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
On January 20th, the U.S. ushered in a new era with President Trump's swearing-in. [The MassGOP celebrated this victory ](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxL90nCYay8sNrpFAt7-FKQnyBgSdd3ll3ik5SXAVw4cJSlAblbkVK_XCeFS19Zs7YP--k2JFyPMrXyVNSA4-li_-kscSofRACkkGPTOmJ-Jz3c-2jssrFhgWFeXY4TGyYlu1gq8Zi09LxMhE3uaSxg5YYHS0Ja2kAbWJwOUA_mPldBdZXFk72yQrlg7kgwsXmcO4MJzcXy6ppfNk-lrcKTw==&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)and the strong message sent by voters: America wants secure borders, a stable economy, and safety at home. Addressing criminal illegal immigration must be a priority. Dozens of residents of Massachusetts traveled to Washington, D.C. to celebrate the ushering in of a new Golden Age. Pictured below is Party Chair Amy Carnevale, National Committeewoman Janet Fogarty and National Committeeman Brad Wyatt.
---
---
| |
---
| Meanwhile, the Healey-Driscoll Administration proposed a reckless $62 billion budget--a 7% increase loaded with new taxes. [We swiftly responded, exposing their plan to burden hardworking families to fund their extreme agenda.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxPsLA6l0P0VBlT71y85Ifdro1UcMsvXPMRdzjIqrl4-HN2lXVDmZXovFloLJhbGaXZaT0I_J58KndBQLf24ydzijuKb7eSX8pmqHHzy3SGy78T87ZyePQZnapoJslsgycbpfAqyDGiDkcffyxyMGRsh5c-FJb3yIvBOIpbDPBcH1SY_wNJA6LJQLeyVNKwH_eO8fpfaze0vTYsODXqZCRRfDCNawmWObbp9lwLiFYW4kcHMUJXlx1eA==&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==) With one-time revenues drying up, ballooning spending is unsustainable, especially amid a strained shelter program and fiscal uncertainty.
Boston has become a hub for criminal illegal immigrant arrests, yet Mayor Wu clings to radical sanctuary policies. Now, Congress is investigating the impact of these policies on public safety. [We've called on Wu to stand by her rhetoric and be held accountable.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxsGbfyPLupfkGTwM_D33A8OLvV3f2qyXS6ysLD9UMmW-MfR1y8tx0w9ae97Qh4dtOnJRszIKoxQHLsEpia-DPSLpJcIOz-5yGvqhubWbV2NFNmk6SZc1XW2bHTExno5IE&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
For years, Democrats have poured billions into the migrant crisis while rejecting Republican-led reforms. Now, the shelter budget has run out again, and Healey wants more taxpayer money. [We refuse to support unchecked spending without concrete reforms to scale back the failed system.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxm-XFyKAkj3ZeWRFOHA1msHEe3pw6cjaKQrcHSnjDrgtAgvMdQUpGxcXvJ6JkDbcXVBiB3wsn-ItzGX3hYAdt-eWmVXdGxwajSM1QqoybKqCv-fU8LCnvIu6E22kt1_O-N1_phWbyyzkxSRvq23_BNgSjrbn5KhuWzSd9r6-dFQzEXYJHMPU--A==&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
As if Healey's tax hikes weren't extreme enough, she's pushing the Municipal Taxation Act, a backdoor tax grab allowing cities to impose new taxes on meals, hotels, and even car ownership. [We will fight this legislation, which directly targets working families.](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxXlkeDO80FG2i2mYxh9OUoen2IsaVXaLCDhNzHwJ2DnpEJIqDHlAM3sDoXMke6SdQE8SY3DJndPaECGxKtblTTaY2gS8io7zUOsRL8BZcKKzvjFsXt7GzplxJgjZ5o0j5_OdMsoIfV0OCN5kl7cRn3vzTFh5fCQNp7SkcosYncOTBLwdDRIfeasXu9Q3usMCXRpPPwlLO9pXwMH64-Pq748ecyy7cBhvELHnnfaqzT3atR-8eZq5Vtg==&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)
MassGOP Chair Amy Carnevale has remained a prominent voice in the Massachusetts media landscape, appearing in numerous articles and interviews over the past month. She recently joined Berkshire Bureau Chief Josh Landes on WAMC and Southcoast Now's Chris McCarthy on 1420 WBSM to discuss the future of the Massachusetts Republican Party and the effort to elect Republicans at all levels.
Listen to Chair Carnevale's full interview with WAMC [HERE](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxFcwi-FQaJ4uO7W-V2B3v3IlMzZbcydL892S8ziF1SthwqBfbPdHCXlMSLBY7QHaLRHpGL9mL6AE-PVKlU13mC0edayGOHUhHCiZq2VJM6-xxUr7QCdCqUd2-aTK0B0_YMm7vN-OP4BoH1LWyDKpZT6eQr0MmW5pWHrsV-8yGxGcSbcL8oXdv8SERCmVzJ9UcK3cgLtsXX7vepVK7wTNTo_62-OFSsIQIe7uOkSHp5_0M7t5lrqVNniBloXSSI4MfC_jDtm3ja_qU6THoMKLN4lcoOHutDX_r6uHoCo9q5vueDC38t4cbOawNMHv1V9bFHX-WVqJ_emcCmfnxblt7tVTUfZekKnD-msbg8yHEyIY85X6OmdPzvWK6PLOX4lo_CBnwAoHPQfRy0yKnrFdWg9azgt27YBic19voeqjJjHbsFmEs3DEH6YQfS-E7hPBF&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)

| State of the Commonwealth Response
Last month, the MassGOP delivered our second State of the Commonwealth Response, presented by Representative Marcus Vaughn of the 9th Norfolk District. In a powerful rebuttal to Governor Healey's address, Rep. Vaughn outlined a vision for moving Massachusetts forward through commonsense reforms and pragmatic leadership. He addressed critical issues, including the migrant crisis, the need for transparency on Beacon Hill, the dangers of one-party rule, and solutions to make Massachusetts more affordable for all. Watch Representative Vaughn's full speech [HERE](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001hOW47C2Ypk1GG9lESee1PZBX1jYyax-TyVoxz9r53T6ZPOrgU6ziWsCFnTH7mzKxZeW86c0iiIoZsnQ4CVsYWAJzDzYodzQ3H-UZNeT7CnshIiq_lHO0VtD6DdTweHALmYQ_POkpblPOYnsEBAzO87egEtIa56FFzCAfFlDV32ThEkAjW4yOC-c0gHOBioZP2E_iEenhji22R3If3A_E4fqCgw6acEktUMLgZjy1CQZNDkmF3SjEmHC8h6sSsCOtCXm8bPzkzyE=&c=hRUYuv1Mstcpmu5nBRjUIaJAce4vbEqyCKJmBk5lkKq6C-2JnHD5bw==&ch=Hix_2OoHONLEY9QG0ZeVFHgfajriG9FzCozK_aAHiTzfR_wxjJcAfw==)!

| RTC Spotlight: Grace Lincoln, Walpole RTC
We proudly honor and thank Grace Lincoln for her steadfast commitment to Republican principles and outstanding leadership as Chair of the Walpole Republican Town Committee. Over the past five years, Grace has significantly expanded the Walpole RTC, fostering a strong, engaged community. She has brought neighboring RTCs together, encouraging collaboration through joint meetings and shared initiatives. Throughout the last campaign cycle, she played a pivotal role in organizing grassroots efforts, ensuring Walpole and Norfolk County remained informed and engaged in standouts, GOTV initiatives, and fundraisers. A dedicated recruiter of candidates and a tireless advocate for our cause, Grace's impact cannot be overstated. We are deeply grateful for her leadership and unwavering dedication to advancing Republican values.

In closing, we cannot thank you enough for your continued support as our momentum grows throughout 2025. We look forward to recruiting strong candidates for municipal elections and building on that success to field competitive candidates for statewide and federal offices in 2026. The MassGOP is strong, and that strength comes from your dedication and hard work!

We hope you've had a great start to the new year and share our enthusiasm for the battles ahead. January was an exciting month as we welcomed a new era of American leadership with President Trump's inauguration and saw our newly elected state legislators sworn in, ready to challenge the Democratic supermajority. Communications This month began with the arrest of an illegal immigrant in a taxpayer-funded shelter, found with firearms and $1 million in fentanyl. House Republicans responded with a letter demanding accountability, and the state's shelter system has wasted billions while fueling a humanitarian crisis--this failure must end. == exposed hundreds of cases of sexual assault, physical assault, child abuse, domestic violence, and drug and human trafficking--all funded by Massachusetts taxpayers. These horrific crimes, hidden by the Healey-Driscoll Administration, occurred in migrant shelters statewide. Governor Healey feigned outrage over the arrest but only ordered shelter inspections after the fact. Our FOIA requests prove her administration knew about these atrocities for years. == State Rep. name: Patriot Alerts
subject: Karoline Leavitt Leaves the Media Speechless

body:
Karoline Leavitt just left the media in awe. They can’t stand how well she defends President Trump.

| | | | [](https://e.updateforamerica.com/rd/9z2zkhhb0tc6vnlg7gm65ic0f6rdaa1oijvljj7a180_rp22sh2s8i6epj0stie8pr4sdh2bs)
---
| | When they try to spin lies about President Trump, she effortlessly casts them aside with the poise & grace we expect out of our Press Secretary.
When asked about President Trump’s harsh language for his adversaries, she responded in a way they never saw coming.
Here’s what she had to say:
**_“I think one of the things that the American people love most about this president is that he often says what they are thinking but sometimes lacks the courage to say themselves. _**
**_And I think yesterday at this podium, you heard President Trump express great frustration, perhaps even anger, with the previous administration’s policies, many of which have led to the crises that our country is currently facing and that this president is focused on fixing.”_**
The far-left media can’t stand that President Trump is angry about what happened to our country under Joe Biden.
They can’t stand that he passionately defends America.
They hate that he PUTS AMERICA FIRST!
But at this critical moment, President Trump’s most ambitious goals are facing attacks from all sides.
In deep-red Florida, Republicans in the legislature are joining hands with woke Democrats to make Florida a de-facto amnesty state.
And in Congress, Republicans are already going on the record to oppose some of President Trump’s most critical cabinet appointees.
**_Facing the Democrats & the media is hard enough, but facing members of our own party is whole different ballgame._**
With Tulsi Gabbard, RFK, and Kash Patel facing Senate confirmation this week, we’re in an all-hands-on-deck situation.
Every Republican needs to stand tall and defend President Trump.
Because if we fail right now, Democrats will use this playbook to thwart President Trump at every turn.
This is the single most important thing you can do today, and we’re coming to you with one humble ask.

name: Rachel
subject: A devotional on humility
body:
In today's episode we talked about God visiting the humble in heart. Here's a devotional to help you get started on your humble journey. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/rachelhamm/a-devotional-on-humility?e=5fa0631807)
---
| |
---
|
## In today's episode of The Rachel Hamm Show I shared that God will be visiting the humble in heart. Here's a devotional to help you get started in choosing to be humble.

The Path of Humility
**Devotional**
In our journey with God, it's easy to rely on our own understanding and wisdom. We often think we know the best path forward, the most efficient route to our destination. But God's ways are higher than our ways, and His thoughts higher than our thoughts. Sometimes, what seems like the most logical path to us isn't the path God has chosen. True wisdom comes when we learn to surrender our understanding and trust in His guidance completely. This requires humility - acknowledging that God's perspective is greater than ours, and His plans are better than anything we could devise on our own. When we humble ourselves before Him, He promises to lift us up and guide us along the path He has prepared. **Bible Verse** "Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight." - Proverbs 3:5-6 **Reflection Question**
In what areas of your life are you currently relying on your own understanding rather than trusting God's guidance?
**Quote from today's episode:** The Lord said that the reason I thought the most efficient path to getting to where God was leading us was traversing up that road was because I was leaning on my own understanding. However, that path would've never led us to our desired destination. **Prayer** Lord, help me to release my grip on my own understanding and trust completely in Your wisdom. Give me the humility to acknowledge that Your ways are higher than mine. Guide my steps today as I submit my plans to You. Amen.

Amen. | | | | | "PLAY TODAY'S EPISODE" | | Did you know that I've written TWO #1 Best-selling books? One of my books is an autobiography where I share my dramatic life story with you. It's a riveting story, full of extreme highs and extreme lows! subject: Five Fascist Acts in 24 Hours—America's Ongoing Blitzkrieg

It is critical we call out this fascism for what it is, document its reality, and stay united to counter its harmful impact

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..lXnjzxxlRWG6rAFPC-KOLC2QnJxGcpyYC7vn9gMuJS4?) for more

* * *
# [Five Fascist Acts in 24 Hours--America's Ongoing Blitzkrieg](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=156521992&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NTIxOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3Mzg3NzQ5MzAsImV4cCI6MTc0MTM2NjkzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.552R-FAfPu4PtGlYdUTcjc1MXRmChckxXGtBgMOc370)
### It is critical we call out this fascism for what it is, document its reality, and stay united to counter its harmful impact
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Feb 5
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=156521992&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NTIxOTkyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczODc3NDkzMCwiZXhwIjoxNzQxMzY2OTMwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.qyiV8eLflnlDTvWog-YvBiyyv5jRmlBildNB3Pn6Nmg&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=156521992&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NTIxOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3Mzg3NzQ5MzAsImV4cCI6MTc0MTM2NjkzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.552R-FAfPu4PtGlYdUTcjc1MXRmChckxXGtBgMOc370&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=156521992&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NTIxOTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3Mzg3NzQ5MzAsImV4cCI6MTc0MTM2NjkzMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.552R-FAfPu4PtGlYdUTcjc1MXRmChckxXGtBgMOc370)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/five-fascist-acts-in-24-hoursamericas?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Last week Connecticut Senator Chris Murphy (correctly) stated that Trump's barrage of Executive Orders was a _blitzkrieg_ attempt to seize control. Blitzkrieg, which translates to Lighting War, was a Nazi strategy to overwhelm and disorient the populace and assume command. Yesterday, in just 24 hours, Donald Trump continued his blitzkrieg on the American people with at least five authoritarian, immoral, and illegal actions that should alarm every American. Each of these acts violates constitutional and international law, yet there has been little to no real pushback from those who should be holding him accountable. While his goal is to leave us confused and exhausted, it is that much more critical we understand what happened, why it's dangerous, and what we must do to stop this descent into full-blown fascism.
**Let 's Address This.**
**Earnest Ask!** I am doing everything in my power to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support. **Subscribe and join 110,000+ fellow activists.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..i0rPlqrx6dO3JUQzZyGHisyFK_wB0oakNGGSLPLwt1E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=156521992)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b66b9bc8-2f48-4c7b-8fca-93af0dd01bce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Democrat Jasmine Crockett speaks as demonstrators rally outside the US treasury department after it was reported billionaire Elon Musk has gained access to the federal payments system. Photograph: Kent Nishimura/Reuters
## 1\. Trump Withdraws the U.S. from the UN Human Rights Council
The United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC) exists to investigate human rights violations and hold abusers accountable. Trump's decision to withdraw the U.S. is not only a blatant endorsement of human rights abuses, but a clear sign that his administration will not tolerate any international scrutiny of its actions.
Withdrawing from the UNHRC is a move straight out of the fascist playbook--isolating the nation from international law to commit atrocities unchecked. The United States already refuses to recognize the ICC or the ICJ. Now, Trump does not want human rights oversight on _any_ aspect of his regime because he knows his regime is actively violating human rights. The withdrawal is an admission of guilt, and a foreshadowing that he intends to violate the rights, not just of non-citizens, but also of U.S. citizens.
## 2\. Trump Declares U.S. Control Over Gaza & Backs Ethnic Cleansing
Trump openly declared that the U.S. would "take over Gaza"--an illegal, colonial, and imperialist act that violates international law. At the same time, he endorsed Israel's ethnic cleansing of Palestinians, confirming that every building in Gaza has been leveled. And to be clear, this also affirms what many lawyers and activists such as myself implored for the last 16 months--Biden's indiscriminate arming of Netanyahu was illegal, and Netanyahu's indiscriminate bombing of Gaza's civilians, hospitals, and schools were war crimes.
Let's be clear: forced displacement is ethnic cleansing. Ethnic cleansing is genocide. The U.S. taking over Gaza is an admission that Netanyahu's war crimes were intentional, coordinated, and backed by the Biden administration and now backed by the Trump administration.
And the hypocrisy is staggering. Trump's base pushes "Stand Your Ground" laws to justify killing anyone who encroaches on their land. But when it comes to brown and Black people, they argue the victims of genocide should simply "leave their ancestral homes" and hand them over to their oppressors.
This is not just about foreign policy. It is about who is allowed to have rights and who is not.
## 3\. Trump Begins Sending Migrants to a Concentration Camp in Guantanamo Bay
February 4th witnessed the first flight of migrants rounded up and flown to be detained in a mass concentration camp in Guantanamo Bay. This is not speculation. This is happening. As I [reported earlier](https://substack.com/redirect/9c98ad4a-f2a9-4cb6-a366-69f6053797e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Trump has ordered building a mass camp in Gitmo to house at least 30,000 migrants.
This move is eerily reminiscent of Executive Order 9066, which led to the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II. It also bears resemblance to Nazi concentration camps, which initially began as detention centers before escalating into something much darker.
And yet, where is the outcry?
Incidentally, February 5 is the 108 year anniversary of The Immigration Act of 1917, which banned Asians, Africans, Latinos, and other non-white people from entering the United States.1 It also banned people with criminal records, people who didn't speak English, and people with disabilities from immigrating. More than a century later this white supremacist policy is almost verbatim Trump's immigration policy. But there's a deeper hypocrisy here.
During that same period of bans on Asian, African, and Latino immigration, European immigrants entered the United States, undocumented and through open borders. One such immigrant during that time was a man who was undocumented, had a criminal record, and was unable to speak English, yet was allowed to enter the United STates anyway. His name was Freidrich Drumpf--grandfather of Donald Trump.2 The staggering hypocrisy of Donald Trump is undeniable. His grandfather was a white undocumented immigrant with a criminal record who did not speak English--yet he was allowed to stay in America. Now, Trump is trying to block non-white immigrants who have no criminal record, do speak English, and came here legally.
And on top of that, he shouts about "merit"--even though he himself has none--while reviving racist policies that punish Black and brown immigrants simply for existing.
This isn't just hypocrisy. This is fascism.
## 4\. Trump Endorses El Salvador's Plan to Imprison U.S. Citizens
Trump, through his unanimously confirmed Secretary of State Marco Rubio, endorsed the El Salvadoran government's plan to imprison U.S. citizens. Yes, you read that right.
The GOP constantly fearmongers about crime and lawlessness, yet they applaud authoritarian regimes imprisoning Americans. The hypocrisy is beyond absurd--it is criminal neglect of U.S. citizens.
Trump constantly screams about "America first." But when it comes to latino American citizens being arrested and detained in El Salvador, "America first" goes out the window. This is another reminder that this slogan has nothing to do with America first and everything to do with putting white supremacy first.
* * *
## 5\. Trump Places U.S. Aid Workers on Permanent Leave
In a final act of cruelty, Trump has removed nearly all U.S. humanitarian aid workers from their posts, cutting off food, water, and medical relief to millions worldwide.
This decision is a direct attack on the world's most vulnerable populations. It means more people will starve. More people will die of preventable diseases. More children will suffer.
And who benefits? War profiteers, military contractors, and corrupt corporate elites who capitalize on human suffering.
This is the same Trump who claims to be a "strong Christian," yet his actions are the very definition of evil.
## What Do These Five Acts Tell Us?
These five actions in a single day should be setting off alarms across the nation. Trump is actively dismantling democracy, endorsing human rights abuses, and laying the groundwork for mass persecution. And if you've read my articles over the last few days, you'd be well aware of the privacy violations, the attacks on DEIA, the dismantling of FAA safety protocols, and the list goes on.
The parallels to historical fascism are undeniable. And history tells us what happens when people ignore the warning signs.
MAGA is not about patriotism. It is not about law and order. It is about power--brutal, unaccountable power.
[Give a gift subscription](https://substack..i0rPlqrx6dO3JUQzZyGHisyFK_wB0oakNGGSLPLwt1E?&gift=true)
## Do Not Let This Be Normalized
If you do nothing else today, remember this: Do not let this be normalized.
This is not how a functioning democracy operates. This is not the country we are supposed to be. This is not acceptable.
We must resist this fascism at every turn.
### Take Action:
✅ [Call your representatives](https://substack.com/redirect/794a043e-0788-4f8d-87c9-98eb3417e766?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--Demand they speak out and take meaningful action against these attempts to advance MAGA fascism. File lawsuits, block hearings, conduct public protests. Do _something_ more than tweeting their disappointment.
✅ Support independent media--Corporate media is normalizing this. Subscribe and share platforms that are accountable to people, [like yours truly](https://substack.com/redirect/18e952a4-48ed-4ba6-85b7-f3f3b3283ab2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), not to shareholders.
✅ Join advocacy organizations--Groups fighting for immigrant rights, Palestinian rights, and democracy need our help. Start local. [I provide seven detailed ideas here](https://substack.com/redirect/9c98ad4a-f2a9-4cb6-a366-69f6053797e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
✅ Stay loud, stay active, stay vigilant--The worst thing we can do is stay silent. Make sure your voice is heard, exercise your 1A rights, and bring your network with you.
I will not stop calling out these injustices. I will not let these horrors be ignored. And I will continue to fight for human rights--but I need and welcome your help.
Join our movement and let's continue the fight for justice, united. Because silence is complicity. And we cannot afford to be complicit.
**Before you go. I am doing everything in my power to stave off fascism under this new administration. I cannot do it alone. Subscribe below and join the movement.**

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: Thank you for standing with me
body:
This could be my last message for awhile

| | | | |
---
Friend, I just attended a full day of events in Upstate New York organized by my supporters to thank me for my decade of service fighting for them in Congress.
To say the display of support humbles me would be an understatement and I want to thank my constituents and the American people from the bottom of my heart.
**_[My staff put together this great video sharing some of the chapters along our journey, and I hope you’ll take a few moments to take a look>>>](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9WMG/?__vbtrk=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_**
[WATCH VIDEO MESSAGE](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9WMG/?__vbtrk=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
If you are reading this email, you are the grassroots patriots that we have to thank for working day and night to save our great nation.
**None of this would have been possible without your hard work and generous financial support.**
I have been called to fight for our America First values in a new role.
But make no mistake, our team’s efforts to preserve the Republican trifecta voters gave to President Trump and defend our nation in Congress from the ruthless attacks of the Left is far from over!
As I write this, Far left special interest groups and Deep State actors are mobilizing Democrat voters across the districts with a special election this year.
This is an all-hands-on-deck moment, and I need the help of my supporters to ensure that every special election this year is won by a conservative Republican who will fight for President Trump’s agenda.
**_[If you’re able, I hope you’ll consider chipping in whatever you can to maintain our majority and help President Trump MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9WMG/?__vbtrk=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_****!**

Thank you for supporting me every step of the way.
It is because of patriots like you that we achieved victory on Election Day and are now able to begin ushering in a New Golden Age for America.
_Your fellow patriot,_
**Elise M. Stefanik**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9WMG/?__vbtrk=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjczODQ0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

subject: This is my third attempt to reach you!
body:
I've been trying to get a hold of you for weeks!

| |

I've been trying to reach you for weeks! My team has been tasked with collecting one million responses to our survey to assess Republican support in 2024 so that we can start 2025 off strong.

name: John Kasich
subject: Have you heard the news?
body:
I wanted you to be among the first to know that I will have a new book out on April 8, 2025!| | | [](http://links.heavenhelpusbook.com/s/c/zIhC3sbtHtCXzrSXLxNo6R5nruHEzb18ZHvZxlpMrvXPtWKYNo79N2yAYjut6PhxP_XbG8H5rVHBs9A9CwaBZRFt8JQOy56KmBtvfRPslo1UtXrI8_TYNskEpjagEyP_AbcrPcYzXkStq3CAVeSY8dzpOSARxnCZP14o3CkvzH-c59sLkbRZ5lMprlWOckQZzELw26WhEElR-RCyS6rO-eAHzd7cJaAg7NE71bmm5o6vr1xFe09-nuk3dqDxEdOEupcHjkwRBcX8Xc1T1cH0v4TrK_UNkQ/aovCA7CYMXKPg7AoDcZ-aj8xpSG41mic/5)
---
| | Peter, One of the most common questions I hear is, **_“How do we turn things around in our country?”_** It's my hope that my new book, [Heaven Help Us](http://links.heavenhelpusbook.com/s/c/X95ONbfTujNZ2F21A2H-Tta_AVbRVUrOjUry-cIHCHdC-8Bjrce70tNXJ9bjyjpfS8b_t5chu5eEFZMWIRao0wWxmO0iziZ1aE_r2OZhcTrfJLTcab91H0zwMYHd5RZGvoc24LwGBuaMAxsK5CqARxVVej5i3l76dqEg_z0Hq1IZqx8_YkEjF2U9-XQioaGfHSXjqqNR6JaJf-b16tWwmqL4Jpfyib7L2M0sCsZ5ZBg422i0nX9VJuBbb3Tkm4D520jg4hp-VBi0YtgnPBumtRGHiPyjMA/eP24FlIwtyWPa0exoyvmYftt901EKowR/5), will help answer this question. It will be released on April 8!

I believe the answer starts with reconnecting to our faith-based communities - our churches, synagogues, mosques, and other houses of worship. These are the places that provide strength, comfort, and a sense of belonging, especially in times of division.

Like each story in my book, my answer to this challenge is about connection, purpose, and hope. Heaven Help Us highlights the extraordinary good that everyday Americans can accomplish when supported by faith-driven communities. This collection of stories will surprise you and fill you with optimism for the future.

I’ve seen firsthand how people, motivated by faith and strengthened by their communities, are achieving great things. Heaven Help Us shines a light on their stories - not just to inspire, but to offer a roadmap for what we can accomplish together.
---

---
[LEARN MORE](http://links.heavenhelpusbook.com/s/c/9kITkqV95AELThtdL5vhIrCr6mhffKiL32e2wyDQIoYOFwzf6fJWPAejlu_GIHhINsF3OtQ6bGF_pjok7SHTIA-RWxwnD_7Dd4oh9G_mD40V-7jhxFzj55eakw8lYG1wzYD56rkbHgda0ZLzg-KPEV-bxItcG2en30ndPUFwkE397ZtOHWVk3nctLkugtV8IgD3CHOKFSrbsnG8IiFCqLQwRPNoNDep9Ei0TTUYnpsM5pD8RXpLaDEE0IgbQXPKe2WOspdjI62B035u-zuakCzUzl39IbQ/qAx6jLglUhTZSpbJ03D65xUIdR1s0jFq/5)

---

---
We'll have plenty more information to share with you as we approach the book launch date.

Thank you for all that you're doing to make a positive difference! -John

name: Eric Sorensen
subject: Official: Hear about my committee roles
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101597376/526471651/1514584251?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmI5OTZlOS1lNWUzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Rh-lB8Pd50VtkwU-1InwKFeOXLcFYSw8YoVC_o5fz8w=)
Peter, Even amid the headlines and chaos, I am keeping my head down and doing everything possible to produce results for Illinois and the 17th District. With that in mind, I’m excited to tell you about my two key committee assignments during the 119th Congress – the House Committee on Agriculture and the House Armed Services Committee. Both posts offer incredible opportunities to make progress here in Illinois’ 17th District and across the country. As you probably know, House committees dictate the content of bills before they’re presented to the House for a full vote. That means I’ll have a strong voice on legislation related to our district’s historical base of military families, active military personnel, and farmers – and that reflects the majority of this country. In other words, these roles offer precisely what IL-17 needs:
* **On the Agriculture Committee:** I’ll prioritize the needs of Illinois farmers – who are not just an engine for our local economies but a key source for the food our residents put on the table. I will make it my mission that we actually pass a Farm Bill, which will have big implications in how we protect our farm families, secure SNAP funding, and advance new rural development.
*
* **On the Armed Services Committee:** Our district is home to the Rock Island Arsenal and the 182nd Airlift Wing at the Peoria Air National Guard Base, and this assignment allows me to ensure these installations and our active service members get the support they need to continue their critical missions.

Progress on these issues is what our district cares about – I’ve heard it directly from many of you while talking in our town halls, office hours, and even in line at the grocery store. It’s also true that delivering gains on these issues has shown, time and again, to be key to our broader efforts of winning back working Americans who feel the party has left them. I’m excited to work, but to stay in a position to make a long-term difference on the issues I’ve discussed here, I’ll need to win re-election in 2026 and help us flip the House so we can set the agenda. That’s where you come in: [**Will you chip in $3 to help me defend this seat so I can continue fighting for our shared values in Congress?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101597380/526471653/1514584251?refcode=em-2.5.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmI5OTZlOS1lNWUzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Rh-lB8Pd50VtkwU-1InwKFeOXLcFYSw8YoVC_o5fz8w=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101597383/526471657/1514584251?refcode=em-2.5.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYmI5OTZlOS1lNWUzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Rh-lB8Pd50VtkwU-1InwKFeOXLcFYSw8YoVC_o5fz8w=)

clean_body: | = Peter, Even amid the headlines and chaos, I am keeping my head down and doing everything possible to produce results for Illinois and the 17th District. With that in mind, I’m excited to tell you about my two key committee assignments during the 119th Congress – the House Committee on Agriculture and the House Armed Services Committee. Both posts offer incredible opportunities to make progress here in Illinois’ 17th District and across the country. As you probably know, House committees dictate the content of bills before they’re presented to the House for a full vote. That means I’ll have a strong voice on legislation related to our district’s historical base of military families, active military personnel, and farmers – and that reflects the majority of this country. In other words, these roles offer precisely what IL-17 needs: On the Agriculture Committee: I’ll prioritize the needs of Illinois farmers – who are not just an engine for our local economies but a key source for the food our residents put on the table. I will make it my mission that we actually pass a Farm Bill, which will have big implications in how we protect our farm families, secure SNAP funding, and advance new rural development. On the Armed Services Committee:** Our district is home to the Rock Island Arsenal and the 182nd Airlift Wing at the Peoria Air National Guard Base, and this assignment allows me to ensure these installations and our active service members get the support they need to continue their critical missions. Progress on these issues is what our district cares about – I’ve heard it directly from many of you while talking in our town halls, office hours, and even in line at the grocery store. It’s also true that delivering gains on these issues has shown, time and again, to be key to our broader efforts of winning back working Americans who feel the party has left them. I’m excited to work, but to stay in a position to make a long-term difference on the issues I’ve discussed here, I’ll need to win re-election in 2026 and help us flip the House so we can set the agenda. name: Office of Delegate Briana D. Sewell
subject: Delegate Briana Sewell's Crossover Update
body:
| |
---
# District 25 Weekly Newsletter
---
## Crossover Update
---
Dear Inquirer,
---
We have officially reached **Crossover** —the final day for each chamber to act on its own legislation, except for budget bills, in order to be considered in the other chamber. With the first four weeks of session behind us and two more to go, I’m proud of the progress we’ve made together.
---
|
---
I’m thrilled to report that **10 of my 14 bills have successfully passed the House of Delegates - 9 of them with unanimous, bipartisan support!** Throughout this session, my office has welcomed constituents, advocacy groups, and young leaders, each sharing their hopes, concerns, and personal stories. As I often say, my job is to **carry the voices of my neighbors to the Capitol** , ensuring that our community’s needs are heard loud and clear. It remains an incredible honor to represent the **25th District** , and I’m excited to update you on our legislative progress. In this email, you’ll find: ✅ A breakdown of where my bills stand
✅ Updates on key legislation moving through the General Assembly
✅ Photos of the incredible constituents and advocates who stopped by to share their stories Thank you for your continued engagement and support—it makes all the difference!
---
|
---
# **2025 Legislative Agenda**
---
It’s been another **busy and productive 28 days** on the legislative front! I’m proud to share that **most of the bills I introduced this session have passed the House** , and for those that didn’t advance, many are being addressed through the efforts of my colleagues. I’m incredibly grateful for the partners, advocates, and constituents who have supported these bills—your voices and engagement make all the difference. If you’d like to speak in support or opposition of any bills in committee, please reach out to **joe@sewellfordelegate.com** for more information. **Below, you’ll find a brief update on the status of the legislation I carried this session.**
---
**Advocating for Veterans and Service Members:**
* [**HB1911 Veterans Services Protection Act**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/eQ2L2LERSiYhngOFPejP_g~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMOUMZ8f-xQn4C7gNAlrlZotMjQW2ZpQ8-zYEK3tAqSs_XRIxbBC9b77NzY-aly72aWqefRf3s2I1wuo2NViP_lA~~)
* Our veterans deserve to receive the full benefits they have earned—free from predatory practices. The Veterans Services Protection Act would prevent unfair charges for Veterans Disability Benefits, ensuring veterans keep every dollar they’re entitled to. Organizations like the Veterans of Foreign Wars and the Virginia Department of Veteran Services already offer these services for free.

_Status: Unfortunately, the House Committee on Labor and Commerce tabled this bill, but I remain committed to protecting our veterans and will continue pushing for this important reform._
* [**HB1880 Military Family IEP Help**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/2w-vKYDoWW-pMrAru7B2VQ~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnM7oFOjKchjr8fBB1rSCa9_8lsEZ638k4g6wQw5SXjDCqSKPkBQvAVbyQn5OKP8H0NF8MS7oXFPOX2qqhKsO66dA~~)
* Military children often face educational disruptions, especially those needing special education services. This bill ensures timely access to Individualized Education Programs (IEPs) when families relocate, reducing unnecessary bureaucratic hurdles.

_Status: I’m happy to report HB1880 passed the House unanimously and is now in the Senate Committee on Education and Health for further consideration and hopeful passage._
* [**HB1881 Military Family School Enrollment Support**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/VqWJdaPgL4RKJkVy9GZk9w~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnM3vxsceaHgoHhDWAS-BDwYrga67LJwurHPBjyOY_vsxA-QbrzvyDaPSGo4id2LUVD5fCRbzUA1pzaLiQvs_R8zQ~~)
* Military families move frequently, but their children’s education shouldn’t be disrupted. HB1881 ensures smoother school transitions by encouraging public school systems to create plans allowing military children to enroll in Virginia public schools, regardless of division boundaries.

_Status: This bill passed the House unanimously and is now under review by the Senate Committee on Education and Health._
* [**HB1882 Military Protective Orders**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/a6m9LCsLdCfQ8eRaXAS2kw~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMvRBm5G6-vNXQkfghfTIKKajuu_OWoAZ7Hc_Sq53YioTtgXwKEmpEEdNQ2SQSCG2MBKSuLFi8yOsJ6JEcShfhaQ~~)
* This bill strengthens protections for service members and spouses who have received Military Protective Orders (MPOs) related to sexual or domestic violence. It ensures that these protections remain in effect when relocating between bases.

_Status: HB1882 passed the House unanimously, and I look forward to continuing this work in the Senate._
* [**HB1685 Military Family Early Childhood Care Fix**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/kpyJfm2wNLBrZ4u38-k_2w~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMLpYk1qJUyCEfu1J9IXrdKjahi_bo2-UL9cK5AB-VQKMABTtofBDIEtj29xCOL3wpFXW5Sv9m1IYlN8Qtlt9o-g~~)
* This bill will expand on my House Bill 732 from the 2024 Legislative Session which was passed with [bipartisan support and signed into law.](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/cUvRdADaEvRZmFhuRE5kNQ~~/AAS2yhA~/rWR_XA_muYW--UTWf-7EtMBp2s1suFnFst1Qnxr9VYFUG5kR_qVnX5DroAn6JX7RPp79O-fuQD6GM_UWHxjbKRBDH9OYZdnT3X9nVhQSAHqYH5fFgePP6qeJjiRdO8T_V8i9Zcv7hawR7ISwRzneH0HZpUBCUcRmmJx4t3TwZGC57b3bS5QbWeICjniHaPDzpRgstV8Ipcmbzg8qR_BtkbMYX9XTjyt4ydUJ5cbnqz6Fyij5yDq4OM5nJXcExOMYssb5nsLmpP2te4C5jHIGpQdzGirZvwkHQzQ39D7aBhQ~) This bill helps Early Childhood Care Centers serving military families avoid duplicative and disruptive administrative requirements.

_Status: I'm happy to report that this bill passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support._ **Investing in Our Children’s Futures:**
* [**HB2530 Shackling Ban for Minors**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/c00BIVmpbJAKqgu33ngsyQ~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMvx3k-3YIknLlbfgGdTbQKdVPplDtsikcYCWvtzKtTFlwKqv56AAbzzuGdd-XhNx9CheTRqBMGBEAZM4jqzhhjA~~)
* Everyone deserves due process and the right to a fair trial, regardless of age or circumstance. This bill seeks to ensure that minors are not subjected to restraints in the courtroom unless it is absolutely necessary to prevent harm, stop disruptive behavior, or ensure safety—when no other less restrictive alternatives are available. As our Commonwealth continues to make strides in supporting the mental and emotional well-being of our children and families, it is essential that we recognize that not every young person who enters the courtroom is a threat to themselves or others. One bad decision, especially in a moment of vulnerability, should not define a child’s future. We must approach these situations with compassion, understanding, and a commitment to helping them learn, heal, and grow.

_Status: This bill has been incorporated into Delegate Cousins’ legislation,_[_House Bill 2222_](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/jD2kNoc0IdotawuvvyOCvA~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMz_2nhKBWoJjvH-DJTnaFbVuBQteuflVXctrrqkZXN_dsR1VeDnsapiScaZCUDr6fV6chJbWMDWxgQX66n5fHwg~~) _, which closely aligns with the bill language I introduced. I appreciate Delegate Cousin’s leadership on this critical issue and look forward to working with her to advance this important initiative._
* [**HB2532 Drone Bill**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/7CDDzEtu1B4iiFnqVzuH5w~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMSddqimw6h6YEHblq45D2Qd4tvz4r-_4algB1KIjTIPL6RYisFZl1fWGWSX5I-j73Lf6aJnqwxx3QAwzHux8U2g~~)
* _To strengthen public safety and emergency response, this bill streamlines the process for law enforcement to obtain search warrants for drone deployment. Drones enhance real-time situational awareness, aid in crime scene assessments, and expedite the delivery of emergency supplies._

_Status: This bill was stricken from the docket due to a lack of full consensus. I sincerely appreciate everyone who supported the legislation and those who raised concerns about potential unintended consequences. Your input will help shape a more effective and balanced proposal when we revisit this issue next year._
* [**HJ436 Sound Camera Study**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/MxK-1wxD9UgiHccflmJNjw~~/AAS2yhA~/mc7H1ezPqIOPZ-gTmCdrqJ5Syk569PVyFquFXweA1ImVsqPXc4sIhA04cqMeUmpjAkeTMGSVy8CoGjOmvjHyLanRSxEXWRNdYHbKeZQ3wYcZMQvlxEdn1JiOW8paL8utTSsmECDCU2YulOxgaeCzRg~~)
* Residents of the 25th District are all too familiar with the persistent nuisance of excessively loud vehicles with modified exhaust systems. What began as an annoyance has grown into a serious safety concern, particularly near our schools. This bill proposes funding a comprehensive study to identify the most effective methods for monitoring and regulating vehicle noise levels. By addressing this issue, we aim to reduce noise pollution and foster a safer, quieter environment for our neighborhoods and schools.

_Status: This bill is one of many pieces of legislation designed to address the same concern. The chosen vehicle to begin studying this persistent concern is_[ _House Bill 2550_](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/38NkqYHt_JzWYEaA-_n6GA~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMjLGRuVcA2ctbjQ5kbyaJsFsiSTN77s6jZeWgvLgr3oIknSXXGhnmu6uah3zhVezTTpyD3QkyMvaQ7y9_MYGj5g~~) _patroned by Delegate Rip Sullivan. The bill will allow localities in the Planning District 8, to include Prince William County, to_ _operate noise abatement monitoring systems to test their impact on recording and enforcing exhaust system violations._
* [**HB2534 DMAS; Crisis Stabilization Services**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/9uWq2XuVfaq9c4iFp1D8gQ~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMBKlZEIsUZ5cIVnjzwNmtyfpheP2ugjKqOF103fBF9EqRyLuxGQqX9yv6UDyrs8a3x2usUzeR5ocDMYNGn1YihQ~~)
* This bill seeks to request a critical waiver to ensure that the soon-to-open Crisis Receiving Center in Prince William County has all the necessary resources to effectively support individuals in mental health crises. By securing this waiver, we can guarantee the center is fully equipped to provide timely and comprehensive care to those in need without further delay.

_Status: I am pleased that this bill has been unanimously approved by the House of Delegates._
* [**HB1878 SCHEV; director title change**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/Kb3Xapbd5IOI0OgiS5TGmw~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMUsFB2pBpbjYU_qhgaEwvzbpqzS4tVsuYdwJCsC9IbBbbVfXcyOJ48_MsrhWXB-j4cLC1_a_Bl56uomlA0VvzbA~~)
* This bill proposes to rename the Director of the State Council of Higher Education for Virginia (SCHEV) to Executive Director. This technical adjustment will streamline internal operations and enhance communication with key partners, while also elevating SCHEV’s role in advocating for our public colleges and universities.

_Status: I'm happy to report that this bill has unanimously passed the House of Delegates and will be considered by the state Senate in the coming weeks._ **Supporting Working Families:**
* [**HB2531 Paid Family Medical Leave**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/k6-RiSkGDqBKxGUd_6V5-Q~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMNNycECTJtve2Ei5PDLQ_Yv56SSDSMDN1YzQFLra6K1aLIT-UnFV1ermD16GH0nS-QjqrdHNWY9pc3gUo5CKuVQ~~)
* In times of crisis, no family should have to choose between caring for a loved one and keeping their job. That’s why I introduced legislation to establish a paid family and medical leave insurance program, giving Virginians the security to take time off for a child’s birth, medical treatment, or caregiving responsibilities - without the fear of lost wages or job loss.

_Status: The bill passed the House of Delegates on a vote of 51 to 47 and is headed to the state Senate for further review._
* [**HB1879 5000 Families**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/rwC7HJzvqRV2JA-B5nwIpA~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMlO1A_F80MmHZdWkmgvGiMUQmlZf-pyVnuzjY3Grz1wm0UeTiGuVCe-ku2zIPpRH1zI-UbBHumcszGNgtbrSi-Q~~)
* The high cost of housing continues to be a problem for Virginians, and housing instability hits families with school-age children the most. The 5,000 Families pilot program would provide rental assistance to qualifying households to help families with children in schools stay where they are, and provide data on how we can address the housing crisis.

_Status: With an $80 million price tag, House Bill 1879 did not make it into the budget. However, we remain committed to working with localities that have established housing trust funds to find sustainable solutions and provide much-needed support to families in need._
* [**HB1683 Dealers; recall costs**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/DvhDtRioaozrBInQZ56WzQ~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMoZ4jEO3u5-ES_W4Oops2NeC1EikTxLbGJT7BhB8Jp4hb6Cgb3gadlPI8scHCU1_H_Ngp31T6Nl0ks_ZGlyMn-A~~)
* This bill would clarify rules for reimbursing car dealers for recall and warranty costs. It also sets a timeline for manufacturers or distributors to notify auto dealers if they are using their right of first refusal on a dealership sale or transfer.

_Status: I am pleased to report that this bill passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support._
* [**HB1684 Licensees engaged by buyers**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/Zpt6icg9LlIRguyAm6dDew~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMhi1iVuW_ZwWNccRMf6YnLhn_47eDmxfFY7lrmhxACRHG9vPAppEsM_uB7L5NHWl3Yt2seZWxCdK-dP139cLxSg~~)
* House Bill 1684 requires real estate licensees working with buyers and tenants to have a written agreement prior to showing the property. Requiring a written agreement protects both the consumer and the licensee by establishing up front what everyone’s obligations are with respect to the work being done on behalf of the buyer. This bill also makes it clear that a real estate licensee representing a seller or landlord may show properties on behalf of their client to a prospective buyer or tenant without signing a representation agreement with that buyer or tenant.

_Status: I am pleased to report this legislation has passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support. Next, it will be heard in the state Senate._
* [**HB2533 Accessory Dwelling Units**](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/B4ttBv1fhW2_oImp57jgtg~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMdyBhBh2gH55sQk26CAAm-Ratw3Sr6WaXJ-BCpm-xUqwAVG3-aQ5jXFtGsMwkvpOqEYAsfL3Yty1BH4ZYNlXe-A~~)
* Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs)—such as fully furnished basements or detached additions—are a valuable tool in addressing the housing affordability crisis. While they are not a standalone solution for expanding housing stock or increasing homeownership opportunities, ADUs provide quality housing options for multigenerational families and others in need of flexible living arrangements. This bill empowers localities to incorporate ADUs into their strategic plans, giving them more options to expand housing availability.

_Status: I’m pleased to share that this bill has passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support and is headed to the state Senate for further consideration._
---
**Bill of Note**
---
Delegate Josh Thomas of the 21st District, located in western Prince William County, introduced [House Bill 1061](https://go.sparkpostmail.com/f/a/DDutN4oEQYDGJA05hg9VDw~~/AAS2yhA~/a5de83zq_ZKycITc8yJ6kb8p1tvh9MlVTLgiWGURslSl6QbgurNMhNHzQVSk5tnMfZbikvCFDWOLHQqm0gwCbzaxaG1Z5mvPcTAWOmZwV7oXmOMgvhHNp0zFL61XTayyy_-4FPAwz8WOfyJd5yUOLw~~) to ensure that special rezoning cases for facilities requiring 100 megawatts or more of power include a study on noise impacts for homes or schools within 500 feet. Additionally, the bill grants localities greater authority to request studies from companies on power usage, water consumption, noise levels, and other local impacts of such facilities. With data center growth sparking significant debate in Prince William County, Delegate Thomas’ bill helps ensure that localities have comprehensive information to make informed land-use decisions. I supported this bill in the Counties, Cities, and Towns Subcommittee #2 -which I chair, in the Counties, Cities, and Towns full committee, and on the House floor. I commend Delegate Thomas for striking a thoughtful balance between local control and transparency in the land-use process.
---
**Recent Visitors in the Office**
---
Many thanks to the members of the Veterans of Foreign Wars, the National Federation of the Blind, Planned Parenthood Action of Virginia, Cancer Action Network, the Medical Society of Virginia, Legal Aid Justice Center, The 40 Strong, the Prince William County Police Association, and countless others for speaking with me and my staff this week. A special thanks to the young members of Prince William County 4-H and the National Future Farmers of America organization for traveling to the state capitol to witness our government in action. Engaging with such bright, curious, and dedicated young leaders is always the highlight of my day!
---

**Page Spotlight**
---

---
I’m especially proud to congratulate Prince Wiggins, a page from our district who I was proud to sponsor. Seeing him thrive and make a positive impact on the team is truly inspiring, keep up the good work Prince!

Thanks again for reading our weekly update and staying in touch with our office. As always, if there is anything we can assist you with, please do not hesitate to call our office at 703-261-4787 or email us at DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov.

clean_body: | | District 25 Weekly Newsletter Crossover Update Dear Inquirer, We have officially reached Crossover —the final day for each chamber to act on its own legislation, except for budget bills, in order to be considered in the other chamber. With the first four weeks of session behind us and two more to go, I’m proud of the progress we’ve made together. | I’m thrilled to report that 10 of my 14 bills have successfully passed the House of Delegates - 9 of them with unanimous, bipartisan support! Throughout this session, my office has welcomed constituents, advocacy groups, and young leaders, each sharing their hopes, concerns, and personal stories. As I often say, my job is to carry the voices of my neighbors to the Capitol , ensuring that our community’s needs are heard loud and clear. It remains an incredible honor to represent the 25th District , and I’m excited to update you on our legislative progress. In this email, you’ll find: ✅ A breakdown of where my bills stand ✅ Updates on key legislation moving through the General Assembly ✅ Photos of the incredible constituents and advocates who stopped by to share their stories Thank you for your continued engagement and support—it makes all the difference! | 2025 Legislative Agenda It’s been another busy and productive 28 days on the legislative front! I’m proud to share that most of the bills I introduced this session have passed the House , and for those that didn’t advance, many are being addressed through the efforts of my colleagues. I’m incredibly grateful for the partners, advocates, and constituents who have supported these bills—your voices and engagement make all the difference. If you’d like to speak in support or opposition of any bills in committee, please reach out to joe@sewellfordelegate.com for more information. Below, you’ll find a brief update on the status of the legislation I carried this session. Advocating for Veterans and Service Members: ~~ Our veterans deserve to receive the full benefits they have earned—free from predatory practices. The Veterans Services Protection Act would prevent unfair charges for Veterans Disability Benefits, ensuring veterans keep every dollar they’re entitled to. Organizations like the Veterans of Foreign Wars and the Virginia Department of Veteran Services already offer these services for free. Status: Unfortunately, the House Committee on Labor and Commerce tabled this bill, but I remain committed to protecting our veterans and will continue pushing for this important reform. ~~ Military children often face educational disruptions, especially those needing special education services. This bill ensures timely access to Individualized Education Programs (IEPs) when families relocate, reducing unnecessary bureaucratic hurdles. Status: I’m happy to report HB1880 passed the House unanimously and is now in the Senate Committee on Education and Health for further consideration and hopeful passage. ~~ Military families move frequently, but their children’s education shouldn’t be disrupted. HB1881 ensures smoother school transitions by encouraging public school systems to create plans allowing military children to enroll in Virginia public schools, regardless of division boundaries. Status: This bill passed the House unanimously and is now under review by the Senate Committee on Education and Health. ~~ This bill strengthens protections for service members and spouses who have received Military Protective Orders (MPOs) related to sexual or domestic violence. It ensures that these protections remain in effect when relocating between bases. Status: HB1882 passed the House unanimously, and I look forward to continuing this work in the Senate. ~~ This bill will expand on my House Bill 732 from the 2024 Legislative Session which was passed with ~ This bill helps Early Childhood Care Centers serving military families avoid duplicative and disruptive administrative requirements. Status: I'm happy to report that this bill passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support. Investing in Our Children’s Futures: ~~ Everyone deserves due process and the right to a fair trial, regardless of age or circumstance. This bill seeks to ensure that minors are not subjected to restraints in the courtroom unless it is absolutely necessary to prevent harm, stop disruptive behavior, or ensure safety—when no other less restrictive alternatives are available. As our Commonwealth continues to make strides in supporting the mental and emotional well-being of our children and families, it is essential that we recognize that not every young person who enters the courtroom is a threat to themselves or others. One bad decision, especially in a moment of vulnerability, should not define a child’s future. We must approach these situations with compassion, understanding, and a commitment to helping them learn, heal, and grow. Status: This bill has been incorporated into Delegate Cousins’ legislation,~~ , which closely aligns with the bill language I introduced. I appreciate Delegate Cousin’s leadership on this critical issue and look forward to working with her to advance this important initiative. ~~ To strengthen public safety and emergency response, this bill streamlines the process for law enforcement to obtain search warrants for drone deployment. Drones enhance real-time situational awareness, aid in crime scene assessments, and expedite the delivery of emergency supplies. Status: This bill was stricken from the docket due to a lack of full consensus. I sincerely appreciate everyone who supported the legislation and those who raised concerns about potential unintended consequences. Your input will help shape a more effective and balanced proposal when we revisit this issue next year. ~~ Residents of the 25th District are all too familiar with the persistent nuisance of excessively loud vehicles with modified exhaust systems. What began as an annoyance has grown into a serious safety concern, particularly near our schools. This bill proposes funding a comprehensive study to identify the most effective methods for monitoring and regulating vehicle noise levels. By addressing this issue, we aim to reduce noise pollution and foster a safer, quieter environment for our neighborhoods and schools. Status: This bill is one of many pieces of legislation designed to address the same concern. The chosen vehicle to begin studying this persistent concern is~~ patroned by Delegate Rip Sullivan. The bill will allow localities in the Planning District 8, to include Prince William County, to operate noise abatement monitoring systems to test their impact on recording and enforcing exhaust system violations. ~~ This bill seeks to request a critical waiver to ensure that the soon-to-open Crisis Receiving Center in Prince William County has all the necessary resources to effectively support individuals in mental health crises. By securing this waiver, we can guarantee the center is fully equipped to provide timely and comprehensive care to those in need without further delay. Status: I am pleased that this bill has been unanimously approved by the House of Delegates. ~~ This bill proposes to rename the Director of the State Council of Higher Education for Virginia (SCHEV) to Executive Director. This technical adjustment will streamline internal operations and enhance communication with key partners, while also elevating SCHEV’s role in advocating for our public colleges and universities. Status: I'm happy to report that this bill has unanimously passed the House of Delegates and will be considered by the state Senate in the coming weeks. Supporting Working Families: ~~ In times of crisis, no family should have to choose between caring for a loved one and keeping their job. That’s why I introduced legislation to establish a paid family and medical leave insurance program, giving Virginians the security to take time off for a child’s birth, medical treatment, or caregiving responsibilities - without the fear of lost wages or job loss. Status: The bill passed the House of Delegates on a vote of 51 to 47 and is headed to the state Senate for further review. ~~ The high cost of housing continues to be a problem for Virginians, and housing instability hits families with school-age children the most. The 5,000 Families pilot program would provide rental assistance to qualifying households to help families with children in schools stay where they are, and provide data on how we can address the housing crisis. Status: With an $80 million price tag, House Bill 1879 did not make it into the budget. However, we remain committed to working with localities that have established housing trust funds to find sustainable solutions and provide much-needed support to families in need. ~~ This bill would clarify rules for reimbursing car dealers for recall and warranty costs. It also sets a timeline for manufacturers or distributors to notify auto dealers if they are using their right of first refusal on a dealership sale or transfer. Status: I am pleased to report that this bill passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support. ~~ House Bill 1684 requires real estate licensees working with buyers and tenants to have a written agreement prior to showing the property. Requiring a written agreement protects both the consumer and the licensee by establishing up front what everyone’s obligations are with respect to the work being done on behalf of the buyer. This bill also makes it clear that a real estate licensee representing a seller or landlord may show properties on behalf of their client to a prospective buyer or tenant without signing a representation agreement with that buyer or tenant. Status: I am pleased to report this legislation has passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support. Next, it will be heard in the state Senate. ~~ Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs)—such as fully furnished basements or detached additions—are a valuable tool in addressing the housing affordability crisis. While they are not a standalone solution for expanding housing stock or increasing homeownership opportunities, ADUs provide quality housing options for multigenerational families and others in need of flexible living arrangements. This bill empowers localities to incorporate ADUs into their strategic plans, giving them more options to expand housing availability. Status: I’m pleased to share that this bill has passed the House of Delegates with unanimous support and is headed to the state Senate for further consideration. Bill of Note Delegate Josh Thomas of the 21st District, located in western Prince William County, introduced ~~ to ensure that special rezoning cases for facilities requiring 100 megawatts or more of power include a study on noise impacts for homes or schools within 500 feet. Additionally, the bill grants localities greater authority to request studies from companies on power usage, water consumption, noise levels, and other local impacts of such facilities. With data center growth sparking significant debate in Prince William County, Delegate Thomas’ bill helps ensure that localities have comprehensive information to make informed land-use decisions. I supported this bill in the Counties, Cities, and Towns Subcommittee #2 -which I chair, in the Counties, Cities, and Towns full committee, and on the House floor. I commend Delegate Thomas for striking a thoughtful balance between local control and transparency in the land-use process. Recent Visitors in the Office Many thanks to the members of the Veterans of Foreign Wars, the National Federation of the Blind, Planned Parenthood Action of Virginia, Cancer Action Network, the Medical Society of Virginia, Legal Aid Justice Center, The 40 Strong, the Prince William County Police Association, and countless others for speaking with me and my staff this week. A special thanks to the young members of Prince William County 4-H and the National Future Farmers of America organization for traveling to the state capitol to witness our government in action. Engaging with such bright, curious, and dedicated young leaders is always the highlight of my day! | | | | | | | | | Page Spotlight | I’m especially proud to congratulate Prince Wiggins, a page from our district who I was proud to sponsor. Seeing him thrive and make a positive impact on the team is truly inspiring, keep up the good work Prince! | Thanks again for reading our weekly update and staying in touch with our office.
