clean_body: The 2025 General Assembly Session | | | | 2025 THE HONEY BADGER REPORT | | | 2025 SESSION Thursday What can you expect? | | ATTEND IN PERSON OR CALL IN TO THE MEETING LIVE | Thursday Senate Committee Meetings | == == == == == == == == == == == == == | AMANDA CHASE | VIRGINIA'S HONEY BADGER | SESSION BEGINS AT 11AM | Dear Hello Now that crossover is behind us, we will only be reviewing House bills. All of the Senate bills that passed the full Senate are now headed to House subcommittees and House committees for deliberation, debate and votes. Vice versa in the Senate. We will only be reviewing House bills in the Senate. Session will begin tomorrow at 11am, but there will not be any bills on 3rd read, so no bills to debate and discuss. There will be bills in committee. That schedule is listed above. I've highlighted a number of controversial or bills of interest that passed the full Senate through crossover. Keep in mind that these bills have only passed the full Senate and still have to pass the full House before heading to the Governor's desk. Most bad bills will pass. Know that. What you should consider is this. What happens next year when Democrats introduce these bills? Will we have a Republican House to vote against these authoritarian bills? Will we have a Republican Governor to veto these bills? Once session is over, we will start mobilizing and impowering you to take action to ensure we win this November. So stay tuned. For now, I'm giving you the tools you need to expose the authoritarian Democrats introduce every year. Knowledge is power. I'm amazed at the number of my Democrat friends who have no idea the crazy bills that their Democrat leadership introduces every year. Notice it's only Democrats introducing all of the abortion bills. Not what Democrats would have you believe. Make a list of all of their bills and talk about them with your Democrat mind friends and neighbors. Most have no idea that their state government wants to dictate to them what car to drive, use their tax payer money to fund abortion, and worse yet terminate the life of a baby born alive. If you're tech saavy, you can use your phone to screen record committee meetings and capture Democrats in their own words. We should promote these on social media. Exactly what I'm going to do once session is over. So stay tuned. For tomorrow, I've provided you with links to bills coming up to review. Just click on the committee meeting name for the full docket. Once you know the name of the committee and find a bill that you want to look into, simply go to the "watch" link below to find the committee name. Remember. You can watch these committees in real time and comment during the public comment time on Zoom from the comfort of your home. Feel free to message me back if you have questions and I will walk you through how to do this. We can't just read friends, we must "do." Tomorrow, the Senate Committee on Education and Health meets at 8:30am. This committee is chaired by Democrat Senator Ghazala Hashmi, who is running for Lieutenant Governor. HB1649 is coming up tomorrow. This bill directs the Board of Medicine to require unconscious bias and cultural competency training as part of the continuing education requirements for renewal of licensure. You've seen this exact same bill before as introduced by a Senate patron. So you know the House version will pass as well. HB1678 is also coming up in Senate Health and Education tomorrow. It requires each local school board to email parents a reminder to secure their prescription drugs and firearms to keep away from children. Once again, you've seen this exact bill introduced by a Senate patron and the bill passed. Chairwoman Hashmi may advise that they have already heard the bill and skip debate, stating that they have already debated this bill. If this happens, she will lead the committee to simply vote on the bill with no further discussion. Unless you're interested in watching Senate speeches called, "points of personal privilege" on the Senate floor, the are no bills on third read to be discussed and debated. You may want to watch the Transportation Committee. Delegate Austin will be introducing a bill to discontinue the requirement and issuance of decals displaying the expiration month and year of motor vehicle registration to be displayed on license plates. It also eliminates the need for rentals to have a special license plate held for rental. If passed, the bill would go into effect July 1, 2026. No docket for the Subcommittee on Public Education has been posted as of 6:30pm. You should be able to watch the subcommittee using the link below. Those are the highlights for tomorrow. | | This afternoon at 12:30pm I had the opportunity to chat with Rob Schilling of the Schilling Show, to provide a legislative update. You can find that interview on the link below. It's about 30 minutes. Generally speaking, I discussed all of the gun control bills the Democrats passed out of the Senate and the Democrats focus on "reproductive (termination) rights," which are nothing more than abortion bills. While it's easy to get discouraged and want to move out of the state, let me suggest this to you. President Trump is exposing the deep state in an unprecedented way in a matter of weeks. The Democrats were receiving taxpayer money through front organizations and NGOs for their campaigns. Now you know why Democrats have had so much money for their campaigns. President Trump, with the help of DOGE and Elon Musk are exposing corruption at a record rate. The truth is finally coming out about Ukraine. Ukraine has been a money laundering operation for Democrats and some Republicans. We're receiving confirmation that NGOs and other front organizations to include the Associated Press, Politico and the New York Times, are all being subsidized by taxpayer dollars. Politico took $34 million from taxpayers. The New York Times received tens of millions more. The Department of Health and Human Services alone put almost $30 million into the deep pockets of the Times, as reported by the publication, the New American. I've never been more optimistic about America. If the money being flooded into the Democrat Party dries up, perhaps we will have a more level playing field this November. Once session comes to an end on Saturday, February 22, I will turn my efforts into bringing you the latest on the clean up of the federal government nationally under the Trump Administration. We are certainly living in exciting times. | IN CASE YOU MISSED IT | | == | | | BILLS THAT PASSED THE FULL SENATE | == Public elementary & secondary schools; student cell phone & smart device possession & use policies. Directs each school board to develop and each public elementary and secondary school to implement age-appropriate and developmentally appropriate policies relating to the possession and use of cell phones by students on school property during regular school hours. The bill requires such policies to (i) restrict, to the fullest extent possible, student cell phone possession and use in the classroom during regular school hours; == Medicine, Board of; continuing education, unconscious bias and cultural competency. Directs the Board of Medicine to require unconscious bias and cultural competency training as part of the continuing education requirements for renewal of licensure. The bill specifies requirements for the training and requires the Board of Medicine to report on the training to the Department of Health and the Virginia Neonatal Perinatal Collaborative. == Reproductive health care services; prohibitions on extradition for certain crimes. Provides that no demand for extradition of a person charged with a criminal violation of law of another state shall be recognized by the Governor if such alleged violation involves the receipt or provision of or assistance with reproductive health care services, as defined in the bill, within the Commonwealth unless the alleged criminal violation would also constitute a criminal offense under the laws of the Commonwealth. The bill also provides that such limit on extradition shall not apply when the person who is accused of an alleged offense in the demanding state was physically present in the demanding state at the time of the commission of the alleged offense and thereafter fled from such state. == Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. Provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member may transfer a firearm owned by such prohibited person to any person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm, provided that such person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm is 21 years of age or older and does not reside with the person who is subject to the protective order. Under current law, there is no requirement that such transferee cannot be younger than 21 years of age and cannot reside with such prohibited person. The bill also provides that such prohibited person who transfers, sells, or surrenders a firearm pursuant to the provisions of the bill shall inform the clerk of the court of the name and address of the transferee, the federally licensed firearms dealer, or the law-enforcement agency in possession of the firearm and shall provide a copy of such form to the transferee. The bill also provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member shall be advised that a law-enforcement officer may obtain a search warrant to search for any firearms from such person if such law-enforcement officer has reason to believe that such person has not relinquished all firearms in his possession. == Elections; election offenses; dissemination of artificial audio or artificial visual media to influence an election; penalty. Prohibits any person from disseminating artificial audio or artificial visual media, as defined in the bill, intended to influence a political campaign that such person knows or should have known is deceptive or misleading. The bill provides that such prohibition shall not apply if such media includes a conspicuous statement that states \"THIS MEDIA DOES NOT REPRESENT A TRUE RECORDING OF THE CANDIDATE\" either for the duration of a visual media or a verbal reading of such statement at the beginning of audio media. The bill provides that any person who fails to include such statement shall be subject to a civil penalty not to exceed $50, but if such person disseminates such artificial audio or artificial visual media being disseminated (i) takes place within 90 days before an election, (ii) is made without the consent of the depicted candidate, and (iii) is made with the intent to injure a candidate or influence the result of an election, such person is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. The bill also provides that any (a) depicted individual, (b) candidate for office who has been injured or is likely to be injured by the distribution of artificial audio or artificial visual media, or (c) entity that represents the interests of voters likely to be deceived by such dissemination shall be entitled to institute an action in the circuit court of the locality where venue would be proper for the prosecution of computer crimes for preventative relief, including an application for a permanent or temporary injunction, restraining order, or other order, against the person who has disseminated the artificial audio or artificial visual media. The bill also provides exceptions for certain content, including when such content constitutes satire or parody. == Health insurance; coverage for contraceptive drugs and devices Requires health insurance carriers to provide coverage, under any health insurance contract, policy, or plan that includes coverage for prescription drugs on an outpatient basis, for contraceptive drugs and contraceptive devices, as defined in the bill, including those available over-the-counter. The bill prohibits a health insurance carrier from imposing upon any person receiving contraceptive benefits pursuant to the provisions of the bill any copayment, coinsurance payment, or fee, except in certain circumstances. == Firearms, certain; age requirement for purchase, penalty. Prohibits any person under 21 years of age from purchasing an assault firearm, with exceptions for the purchase of an assault firearm by a law-enforcement officer, correctional officer, jail officer, or member of the Armed Forces of the United States, the Virginia National Guard, or the National Guard of any other state. Accordingly, the bill prohibits a licensed dealer from selling, renting, trading, or transferring from his inventory an assault firearm to any person under 21 years of age. A violation of either prohibition is a Class 6 felony. The bill also expands the definition of assault firearm as the term applies to criminal history record information checks. == Assault firearms; carrying in public areas prohibited, penalty. Prohibits the carrying of certain semi-automatic center-fire rifles and shotguns on any public street, road, alley, sidewalk, or public right-of-way or in any public park or any other place of whatever nature that is open to the public, with certain exceptions. Under current law, the current prohibition on carrying certain shotguns and semi-automatic center-fire rifles and pistols applies to a narrower range of firearms, only in certain localities, and only when such firearms are loaded. == Plastic firearms or receivers, unserialized firearms, etc.; transfer, etc., prohibited, penalties. Creates a Class 5 felony for any person who knowingly manufactures or assembles, imports, purchases, sells, transfers, or possesses any firearm that, after removal of all parts other than a major component, as defined in the bill, is not detectable as a firearm when subjected to inspection by the types of detection devices, including X-ray machines, commonly used at airports, government buildings, schools, correctional facilities, and other locations for security screening. The bill updates language regarding the types of detection devices that are used at such locations for detecting plastic firearms. Under current law, it is unlawful to manufacture, import, sell, transfer, or possess any plastic firearm and a violation is punishable as a Class 5 felony. == Firearms; purchase, etc., assault and battery of family member or intimate partner, penalties. Adds to the existing definition of "family or household member" a person's intimate partner, defined in the bill as an individual who, within the previous 12 months, was in a romantic, dating, or sexual relationship with the person. The bill also provides that any person who knowingly and intentionally purchases, possesses, or transports any firearm following a misdemeanor conviction for an offense that occurred on or after July 1, 2025, for the offense of assault and battery against an intimate partner or an offense substantially similar under the laws of any other state or of the United States is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. == Trigger activator; definition does not include semi-automatic replacement trigger, etc., penalty. Defines \\\"trigger activator\\\" as a conversion kit, tool, accessory, or device designed to alter the rate of fire of a semi-automatic firearm to mimic automatic weapon fire or used to increase the rate of fire to a rate faster than such semi-automatic firearm can achieve when not equipped with a conversion kit, tool, accessory, or device. The bill clarifies that \"trigger activator\" includes a bump stock, trigger crank, hellfire trigger, binary trigger system, burst trigger system, or a copy thereof, but does not include a semi-automatic replacement trigger, designed for use in competitive shooting, that improves performance and functionality over the stock trigger. == Firearms; five day waiting period, penalty. Provides that no person shall sell a firearm unless at least five days have elapsed from the time the prospective purchaser completes the written consent form to have a licensed dealer obtain criminal history record information, with exceptions enumerated in relevant law. == Virginia Sports Tourism Fund; definitions, created, report. Creates the Virginia Sports Tourism Fund, to be Administered by the Virginia Tourism Authority, to provide grants to sports tourism activity sponsors, defined in the bill, in order to develop and attract sports tourism activities, also defined in the bill, to the Commonwealth. The bill requires the Virginia Tourism Authority to establish guidelines and criteria for grant awards and to report annually on the Fund and grant program. The bill also requires a work group of stakeholders to be convened to recommend guidelines for grant disbursements from the Fund and report such recommendations to the General Assembly by October 31, 2025. == Cannabis control; establishes framework for creating retail market, penalties. Establishes a framework for the creation of a retail marijuana market in the Commonwealth, to be administered by the Virginia Cannabis Control Authority. The bill allows the Authority to begin issuing all marijuana licenses on September 1, 2025, but provides that no retail sales may occur prior to May 1, 2026. == Casino gaming; eligible host localities. Adds Fairfax County to the list of localities eligible to host a casino in the Commonwealth and provides that any proposed site for a casino gaming establishment considered by Fairfax County shall be (i) located within one-quarter of a mile of an existing station on the Metro Silver Line, (ii) part of a coordinated mixed-use project development consisting of no less than 1.5 million square feet, (iii) within two miles of a regional enclosed mall containing not less than 1.5 million square feet of gross building area, and (iv) outside of the Interstate 495 Beltway. == Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds, etc. Prohibits any person from converting contributions to a candidate or his campaign committee for personal use. Current law only prohibits such conversion of contributions with regard to disbursement of surplus funds at the dissolution of a campaign or political committee. The bill provides that a contribution is considered to have been converted to personal use if the contribution, in whole or in part, is used to fulfill any commitment, obligation, or expense that would exist irrespective of the person's seeking, holding, or maintaining public office but allows a contribution to be used for the ordinary and accepted expenses related to campaigning for or holding elective office, including the use of campaign funds to pay for the candidate's dependent care expenses that are incurred as a direct result of campaign activity. The bill provides that any person subject to the personal use ban may request an advisory opinion from the State Board of Elections on such matters. The bill directs the State Board of Elections to adopt emergency regulations similar to those promulgated by the Federal Election Commission to implement the provisions of the bill and to publish an updated summary of Virginia campaign finance law that reflects the State Board of Elections' and Attorney General's guidance on the provisions of such law that prohibit the personal use of campaign funds and any new regulations == Elections; conduct of election, ranked choice voting, locally elected offices, report. Allows elections for any local office to be conducted by ranked choice voting. The bill requires the State Board of Elections to provide standards for and to approve vote tabulating software for use with existing voting systems in elections conducted by ranked choice voting and to produce generalized voter education materials on ranked choice voting. The bill permits the State Board to create and modify recount procedures to the extent necessary to accommodate a recount of an election conducted by ranked choice voting. Finally, the bill directs the Department of Elections to review the testing and approval framework for voting equipment in the Commonwealth and submit a report of such review no later than the first day of the 2026 Regular Session of the General Assembly. == Elections; general registrar of each locality to report number of provisional ballots cast. Requires the general registrar of each locality to report to the Department of Elections for each precinct in his locality the total number of provisional ballots cast, the number of those provisional ballots determined to be valid by the electoral board, and the results of such valid provisional ballots by voters assigned to such precinct. Results from provisional voting and voting at the precinct on election day must be reported separately. The Department shall establish standards for ascertaining and reporting such information. Such standards must direct that all results be posted promptly and no later than 5:00 p.m. on the tenth day after the election day. == School board policies; parental notification, safe storage of prescription drugs and firearms, etc. Requires each local school board to develop and implement a policy to require the annual notification of the parent of each student enrolled in the local school division, to be sent by email and, if applicable, SMS text message within 30 calendar days succeeding the first day of each school year, of (i) the importance of securely storing any prescription drug, as defined in relevant law, present in the household and (ii) the parent's legal responsibility to safely store any firearm present in the household. The bill requires each school board to make such parental notification available in multiple languages on its website. == Virginia Reproductive and Gender-Affirming Health Care Protection Act. Provides that no demand for extradition of a person charged with a criminal violation of law of another state shall be recognized by the Governor if such alleged violation involves protected health care activity within the Commonwealth unless the alleged criminal violation would also constitute a criminal offense under the laws of the Commonwealth. Protected health care activity is defined in the bill as the provision or receipt, attempted provision or receipt, or assistance or attempted assistance in the provision or receipt of any health care that is lawful in the Commonwealth by a health care provider licensed under the laws of the Commonwealth and physically present in the Commonwealth. == Contraception; establishes right to obtain, enforcement. Establishes a right to obtain contraceptives and engage in contraception, as defined in the bill. The bill creates a cause of action that may be instituted against anyone who infringes on such right. == Weapons; possession prohibited in a hospital that provides mental health or developmental services. Makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person to knowingly and intentionally possess in the building of any hospital that provides mental health services or developmental services in the Commonwealth, including an emergency department or other facility rendering emergency medical care, a (i) firearm, (ii) knife with a blade over three and one-half inches, or (iii) other dangerous weapon, including explosives and stun weapons. The bill also provides that notice of such prohibitions shall be posted conspicuously at each public entrance of any hospital and no person shall be convicted of the offense if such notice is not posted, unless such person had actual notice of the prohibitions. The bill provides that any such firearm, knife, explosive, or weapon shall be subject to seizure by a law-enforcement officer and forfeited to the Commonwealth and specifies exceptions to the prohibition. == Firearms; storage in residence where minor or person prohibited from possessing is present, penalty. Requires any person who possesses a firearm in a residence where such person knows that a minor or a person who is prohibited by law from possessing a firearm is present to store such firearm and the ammunition for such firearm in a locked container, compartment, or cabinet that is inaccessible to such minor or prohibited person. The bill provides that a violation is a Class 4 misdemeanor. The bill exempts (i) any person in lawful possession of a firearm who carries such firearm on or about his person and (ii) the storage of antique firearms and provides that the lawful authorization of a minor to access a firearm is not a violation of the bill's provisions. The bill also requires firearm dealers to post a notice stating such firearm storage requirements and the penalty for improperly storing such firearms. == Assault firearms/certain ammunition feeding devices; purchase, etc., prohibited, exception, penalty. Creates a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person who imports, sells, manufactures, purchases, possesses, transports, or transfers an assault firearm, as that term is defined in the bill, and prohibits a person who has been convicted of such violation from purchasing, possessing, or transporting a firearm for a period of three years from the date of conviction. The bill provides that an assault firearm does not include any firearm that is an antique firearm, has been rendered permanently inoperable, is manually operated by bolt, pump, lever, or slide action, or was manufactured before July 1, 2025. The bill also prohibits the sale of a large capacity ammunition feeding device, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill provides that any person who willfully and intentionally (i) sells an assault firearm to another person or (ii) purchases an assault firearm from another person is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor and that any person who imports, sells, barters, or transfers a large capacity ammunition feeding device is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor and exempts members of cadet corps that are recognized by a public institution of higher education while such member is in the performance of lawful military training or participating in an official ceremonial event for the Commonwealth. The bill also makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person younger than 21 years of age to import, sell, manufacture, purchase, possess, transport, or transfer an assault firearm regardless of the date of manufacture of such assault firearm and exempts members of cadet corps that are recognized by a public institution of higher education while such member is in the performance of lawful military training or participating in an official ceremonial event for the Commonwealth. == Capitol Sq. or building owned/leased by the Commonwealth, carrying firearm/explosive material, etc. Permits the governing board of a public institution of higher education to adopt a policy prohibiting the carrying of any firearm, ammunition, or components or combination thereof within any building owned or operated by such public institution of higher education. The bill allows such policy to include security measures that are designed to reasonably prevent the unauthorized access of buildings that are open to the public. Finally, the bill exempts certain activities, defined in the bill, operated at public or private institutions of higher education from any policy created by a governing board. == Carrying concealed weapons; exception, penalty. Removes the exception to the prohibition on carrying a concealed weapon for any person who may lawfully possess a firearm and is carrying a handgun while in a personal, private motor vehicle or vessel and such handgun is secured in a container or compartment in the vehicle or vessel. == Firearm industry members; creates standards of responsible conduct, civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members and requires such members to establish and implement reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, sale, distribution, use, and marketing of the firearm industry member's firearm-related products, as those terms are defined in the bill. Such reasonable controls include reasonable procedures, safeguards, and business practices that are designed to (i) prevent the sale or distribution of a firearm-related product to a straw purchaser, a firearm trafficker, a person prohibited from possessing a firearm under state or federal law, or a person who the firearm industry member has reasonable cause to believe is at substantial risk of using a firearm-related product to harm themselves or unlawfully harm another or of unlawfully possessing or using a firearm-related product; (ii) prevent the loss of a firearm-related product or theft of a firearm-related product from a firearm industry member; (iii) ensure that the firearm industry member complies with all provisions of state and federal law and does not otherwise promote the unlawful manufacture, sale, possession, marketing, or use of a firearm-related product; and (iv) ensure that the firearm industry member does not engage in an act or practice in violation of the Virginia Consumer Protection Act. The bill also provides that a firearm industry member may not knowingly or recklessly create, maintain, or contribute to a public nuisance, as defined in the bill, through the sale, manufacturing, importing, or marketing of a firearm-related product. The bill creates a civil cause of action for the Attorney General or a local county or city attorney to enforce the provisions of the bill or for any person who has been injured as a result of a firearm industry member's violation to seek an injunction and to recover costs and damages. == Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference). == Constitutional amendment (first reference); qualifications of voters; right to vote; persons not entitled to vote. == Constitutional amendment; marriage between two adult persons, etc., (first reference). | == == == | In the Virginia Senate, each bill has three Constitutional reads. Only bills on the third read and are on the "regular calendar" are debated. Bills that are on the "uncontested calendar," are bills that all Democrat and Republican Senators gave a thumbs up. So, when the bill comes to the Senator floor from the committee, unless a Senator pulls the bill out of the block, the bill is not debated on the floor and is simply voted on in a block. As a Senator, I always reviewed the bills on third read on the uncontested calendar and many times would pull a bill out of the block, to hear the patron present the bill, to ensure I was in agreement with the members of the committee. I've only highlighted bills on the third read for your convenience. | | | | | | | | | IMPORTANT DATES | 2025 LEGISLATIVE SESSION IMPORTANT DATES Begins: Wednesday, January 8 at noon Crossover: Tuesday, February 4 Ends: Saturday, February 22 Last Day for Governor's Actions: Monday, March 24 Reconvene: Wednesday, April 2 | IMPORTANT LINKS | | == | | == | | == | == | | == | SUPPORT AMANDA HERE | While this publication is free, your contributions are greatly appreciated. I served 8 years in the Virginia Senate from 2016-2023 and want to put that experience and knowledge to work for you. I know how things really work in Richmond and I want you to know too, so that you can hold your elected representatives accountable. A huge thanks to those of you who have already chipped in. Thank you. We must now save Virginia. Let's do this. | | == | | == | == ---|--- == | == ---|--- == | == ---|--- | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 250 | Appomattox, VA 24522 US | | | | | | |

name: Action Alert, AOTL
subject: URGENT: Stop the extreme SAVE Act
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
The SAVE Act is a threat to our democracy; take action NOW!
|
---

Peter,
Extremists in Congress are pushing an extreme anti-voter bill – the SAVE Act – that would make it harder, if not impossible, for millions of voters across the country to register to vote. **We need your help to stop it.**
The SAVE Act would force every voter to provide a passport or birth certificate every time they register or update their voter registration and would effectively end online and mail-in voter registration.
This would disproportionately impact military voters, rural voters, tribal citizens, survivors of natural disasters, working-class Americans, and the tens of millions of married women who have changed their names. About half of American citizens _**– nearly 150 million citizens –**_ do not have a passport, and elderly Americans are one of the demographics least likely to have a passport.
This bill isn’t about election integrity. It’s about silencing voters. There’s _zero evidence of widespread voter fraud,_ and states already have secure systems in place to verify voter eligibility. **The real goal of the SAVE Act? To make it harder for eligible Americans to vote.**
**We can’t let them get away with this.[Click below to send an email to your representatives in Congress and demand they vote NO on the SAVE Act.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101607660/526496950/395572353?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_SAVEAct_email_20250205-AOTL-Tier1-SAVEAct_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20250205-AOTL-Tier1-SAVEAct_&utm_campaign=SAVEAct&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk3MjMxNDZiLWYzZTMtZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=1EMl4QHeIA8oQzQeHokAm32aoDrwW4krfvrqd0TFrJI=&emci=ebde6a99-4de3-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&emdi=9723146b-f3e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=8431045)**

body:
Last week, we won in a district Donald Trump carried by 21 points. This is our fourth special election victory this year and sends a powerful message to extreme Republicans in Washington. | | |
---
| |
---|---
Last week, Democrats in Iowa flipped a state Senate seat in a Trump +21 district. _Yes, you read that right_ – we won in a district Donald Trump carried by 21 points. This is our fourth special election victory this year and sends a powerful message to extreme Republicans in Washington. Our party is proving that when we invest in the states, we can win even in Republican-controlled districts. With more special elections coming up in the next few months, we need your help to keep our momentum going. [Will you make a $7 donation to help Democrats capitalize on this moment and win tough races everywhere? ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu05XtZnrBMT5R_mtAuAjxDBs5M1B9a8Y0-8wJfrZEwEEi73POeJR9UgJX3L25ra0s3lzU5xLrNswJR6_xA0RYZT_e8FtdyAMy_vk1WtF_QjinDdO6jwJEzkPKB9LwZ7bOea31BIBrZOmuS_cmx7zeI3SayPKg9KVvb9C6FdPAgMJFZyf1E6TVVdi8z5RKTRxEEJ6MrMWBOYVcgnVaSmenaLtrCuMu6UWCo7komvHjmRbMlYmQKJtEExUsOrS9tfQ9pqFpfebTEaapAJUwnnEo7VYoPHSlYulhNIHiKui9y21Zi_AQH9d1XkZKunBVLglubWc4jLGXX8mRTQuXLEMvVOnHqW_D2yW1eRbhWI84Epg/4dr/8Vd_jQO0T2q99jFpvme-Kg/h0/h001.9EhaAO3w7T57HOz_55kecF-7_DgaySzcrA79odMaeck) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately.

name: Fair Fight
subject: A serious threat to election security
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight
body:
This power grab must be stopped |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101609267/526499973/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250204_f_FedFundingFreeze_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTY3ODAyYi1mNmUzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9h6RdRcIUikwBy-_Aq8i7n69kKo92zxI0cSyaNlLeVA=&emci=23294a25-f6e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=d567802b-f6e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=1037277)

| | Buried within the Trump administration’s recent order to pause federal funds was a sneaky attack on election security. The freeze targeted $55 million in Election Security Grants administered by the U.S. Election Assistance Commission. **Cutting off election security funding isn’t just irresponsible — it actively weakens America’s ability to protect our democracy.** The Trump administration rescinded its order following a federal court’s temporary block, but they continue to fight the block in their attempt to gut programs they deem unworthy and move forward with their extremist agenda. These funds are essential for upholding election security and modernizing voting infrastructure. The administration’s attempt to withhold these resources is a calculated move that reflects Project 2025’s goal of undermining trust in the electoral process, including weakening our elections’ cybersecurity protections. This funding freeze tried to pull the plug on election security and inject chaos into our elections. Gutting bipartisan election security funds after we saw the 2024 election targeted by a level of foreign threats that surpassed both 2016 and 2020 is a reckless decision. Even though the administration’s effort to undermine election independence failed for now, stopping Trump’s power grab before it sticks is essential to protecting independent elections, and by extension, all of our freedoms. [**We can’t sit by and let this happen. We must stay alert and aware of the sneaky actions of this administration. Fair Fight was created to expose anti-voter activity, and that’s what we’ll continue to do with your support.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101609268/526499974/-1125869122?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250204_f_FedFundingFreeze_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTY3ODAyYi1mNmUzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9h6RdRcIUikwBy-_Aq8i7n69kKo92zxI0cSyaNlLeVA=) While the courts have taken initial steps to block this freeze, Congress must act swiftly to reassert its constitutional power, check presidential overreach, and protect our elections from permanent damage. This freeze sets a dangerous precedent for unchecked executive power, and a threat to our rights is something we take very seriously. Thank you for helping us protect our rights. clean_body: This power grab must be stopped | | | Buried within the Trump administration’s recent order to pause federal funds was a sneaky attack on election security. The freeze targeted $55 million in Election Security Grants administered by the U.S. Election Assistance Commission. Cutting off election security funding isn’t just irresponsible — it actively weakens America’s ability to protect our democracy. The Trump administration rescinded its order following a federal court’s temporary block, but they continue to fight the block in their attempt to gut programs they deem unworthy and move forward with their extremist agenda. These funds are essential for upholding election security and modernizing voting infrastructure. The administration’s attempt to withhold these resources is a calculated move that reflects Project 2025’s goal of undermining trust in the electoral process, including weakening our elections’ cybersecurity protections. This funding freeze tried to pull the plug on election security and inject chaos into our elections. Gutting bipartisan election security funds after we saw the 2024 election targeted by a level of foreign threats that surpassed both 2016 and 2020 is a reckless decision. Even though the administration’s effort to undermine election independence failed for now, stopping Trump’s power grab before it sticks is essential to protecting independent elections, and by extension, all of our freedoms. = While the courts have taken initial steps to block this freeze, Congress must act swiftly to reassert its constitutional power, check presidential overreach, and protect our elections from permanent damage. This freeze sets a dangerous precedent for unchecked executive power, and a threat to our rights is something we take very seriously. Thank you for helping us protect our rights. Friends, As you know, I am the Democrat running in the FL-06 Special Election which is set to take place on April 1st. Whereas in most normal special elections for vacant Congressional seats, the Governor in the state will give campaigns an average of six months to build their campaigns and introduce themselves to voters. Florida’s Governor Ron DeSantis has compressed this window into around 4 months to give us the shortest amount of time to build out our campaign while the MAGA movement dumps tens of millions into our district, saturating it with ads in hopes of shooting in their ultra MAGA pick: Randy Fine. In recent days, the _**full frontal MAGA assault on my district**_ entered its newest phase. A pro-Trump super PAC began flooding our district with this ad in support of our opponent: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101609881/526501268/-423390815?refcode=250205_Serve_Weil_GiantMAGAads&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDYwMWIyZi1mN2UzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7l8r3mbZIAHJz0fwdI-LKnRYGe9gSSdqt8XwmVX4IA=) _The ad that Defend American Jobs - a MAGA aligned super PAC began running in my district yesterday._ These ads are beyond critical to my opponent's path to victory because _**no one**_ in this district __**knows him**__. He is a carpetbagging Casino mogul from South Florida. If he was a true shoo-in, MAGA would keep its powder dry and wouldn’t feel the need to pump millions into this district. The fact that they are having to saturate the district in ads to save their candidate, is extremely promising for us _**but only if we can keep up with their spends**_ in the _few weeks_ left until E-Day. So far we are rolling with the punches and are closing in on having raised $500,000 since we began fundraising in late December but we need to scale up faster. **If just 1 out of every 20 people we send this email to donate $5 we could raise 100k off of a single email and run more of our own ads to keep up.[Can you help us keep up with the dark money pro-MAGA PACs flooding into this race by making a donation of $5 or more split with Serve America and me?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101609882/526501269/-423390815?refcode=250205_Serve_Weil_GiantMAGAads&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDYwMWIyZi1mN2UzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7l8r3mbZIAHJz0fwdI-LKnRYGe9gSSdqt8XwmVX4IA=)** [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101609883/526501270/-2031701896?refcode=250205_Serve_Weil_GiantMAGAads&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDYwMWIyZi1mN2UzLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R7l8r3mbZIAHJz0fwdI-LKnRYGe9gSSdqt8XwmVX4IA=) That MAGA needs to flood the airwaves so early on in this race is a good sign for us - with your support we’ll soon be flooding the airwaves with ads of our own. Thank you in advance for your support. Josh Weil

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Trump: “The U.S. will take over the Gaza Strip… we’ll own it.”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Standing side by side with Israeli Prime Minister and war criminal
Benjamin Netanyahu, Donald Trump made it explicitly clear yesterday:

He intends to help Israel carry out the ethnic cleansing of Palestinians
and take over the Gaza Strip.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250205?refcode=em20250205-36398&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36398_1203867_nebXj2&akid=36398%2E1203867%2EnebXj2 ]A sreenshot of a post on X from @prem_thakker. The post has a photo of
Trump and Netanyahu meeting at the White House with the caption: One of
those clips where you have to make sure you're not hallucinating. Donald
Trump says Palestinians ought not be allowed to return to Gaza because
'why would they want to return? That place has been hell' — next to the
grinning man who made it hell.

After the U.S. government spent the last year and a half providing
billions of dollars of bombs and weapons while ignoring federal and
international law to enable Israel’s genocide against the Palestinian
people, Trump plans to help Netanyahu finish the job by forcing
Palestinians out of their land and establishing a U.S. occupation.

This news comes the same week the Israeli government once again violated
its ceasefire agreement by dropping bombs on Palestinian civilians making
the return journey back to what’s left of their homes. At the same time,
while Donald Trump and Elon Musk attempt to slash federal government
spending across the board, they’re planning on sending another $1 billion
in taxpayer dollars to Israel.

We will not sit by while Trump and Netanyahu plan to purge Palestinians
from their communities to turn Gaza into Israeli and U.S. real estate
developments. Justice Democrats members like Rep. Rashida Tlaib, the only
Palestinian American member of Congress, are speaking out:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250205?refcode=em20250205-36398&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36398_1203867_nebXj2&akid=36398%2E1203867%2EnebXj2 ]A post on X from Rashida Tlaib. It is a repost from the Associated
Press reading: BREAKING: President Trump says he wants the U.S. to take
ownership of the Gaza Strip after resettling Palestinians in other
countries. Rashida Tlaib said: Palestinians aren't going anywhere. This
president can only spew this fanatical bullshit because of bipartisan
support in Congress for funding genocide and ethnic clensing. It's time
for my two-state solution colleagues to speak up.

But we need more courageous, pro-peace leaders to join the rest of our
Justice Democrats bloc in Congress and fight for Palestinian human rights.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250205?refcode=em20250205-36398&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36398_1203867_nebXj2&akid=36398%2E1203867%2EnebXj2 ]Help us recruit and elect more working-class, progressive champions
like Rashida who will fiercely fight Trump and Netanyahu’s ethnic
cleansing: make a grassroots contribution now to directly fuel our work.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250205?refcode=em20250205-36398&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36398_1203867_nebXj2&akid=36398%2E1203867%2EnebXj2 ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250205-36398-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36398_1203867_nebXj2&akid=36398%2E1203867%2EnebXj2

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36398%2E1203867%2EnebXj2

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

It is a repost from the Associated Press reading: BREAKING: President Trump says he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip after resettling Palestinians in other countries. Rashida Tlaib said: Palestinians aren't going anywhere. This president can only spew this fanatical bullshit because of bipartisan support in Congress for funding genocide and ethnic clensing. It's time for my two-state solution colleagues to speak up. But we need more courageous, pro-peace leaders to join the rest of our Justice Democrats bloc in Congress and fight for Palestinian human rights. [ ]Help us recruit and elect more working-class, progressive champions like Rashida who will fiercely fight Trump and Netanyahu’s ethnic cleansing: make a grassroots contribution now to directly fuel our work. [ ]Contribute $5 In solidarity, Justice Democrats Contribute to Justice Democrats:

clean_body: This is a unique opportunity to reform our government. Peter, I have an important update to share with you. Now that Republicans control the Senate majority, I have become the Chairman of the Permanent Subcommittee on Investigation. I ran for the Senate because I wanted to bring back transparency and build a government that is ruled by the American people, not by bureaucrats or corporations. As Chairman, I intend to do just that. But here's the truth - We will face a lot of resistance in my mission to restore integrity, and I need you to stand by me. Your support will power my mission to restore integrity and transparency to the federal government. ********** ******** ******** ********** This mission is of utmost importance, and your backing is crucial. Thank you for your commitment, Chairman Ron Johnson Paid for by Ron Johnson for Senate Inc. %%subscriptioncenterurl%% PO Box 1159 Oshkosh, WI 54903

---
**The Trump administration is offering buyouts to the entire CIA workforce, the same way he has targeted other federal jobs within the last two weeks. ** Greetings from Gay Valimont, FL-6 Dem candidate. CIA Director John Ratcliffe has joined the MAGA armada and is including freezing hiring and potentially rescinding conditional offers to applicants who don't fit the “new focus areas.” We have already seen the agenda this administration has carried into office, and this move represents a broader threat. **I need your help ASAP ; Special elections are around the corner and we cannot wait to gather support.**
---
---
**This administration's broader efforts to downsize federal agencies and install loyalists further exacerbate concerns about the politicization of intelligence operations.** As someone deeply committed to safeguarding our nation's security and the integrity of our institutions, I find these developments alarming. The expertise and nonpartisan dedication of our intelligence community are vital to informed decision-making and effective policy implementation.

This administration's broader efforts to downsize federal agencies and install loyalists further exacerbate concerns about the politicization of intelligence operations. As someone deeply committed to safeguarding our nation's security and the integrity of our institutions, I find these developments alarming. The expertise and nonpartisan dedication of our intelligence community are vital to informed decision-making and effective policy implementation. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = Your support is crucial in this endeavor. Sincerely, Gay Valimont Democratic Candidate for Florida’s 1st Congressional District | | | | | Paid For By Gay Valimont Gay Valimont For Congress PO Box 1258 Gulf Breeze, FL 32562 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: JanePAC
subject: ✊ The resistance showed up yesterday.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
We need more of this from Democrats in Congress.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101611369/526506358/1328794144?refcode=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjlkNDZhNTI5LWZiZTMtZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=cAUto9eouJPfsLxOxMu5-TTn4LG-ZXAHj7HLxy7MQeo=&emci=add14f96-f4e3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=4823105)
---
Yesterday, our endorsed leaders stepped up. Reps. Maxwell Frost, Jasmine Crockett, and Sydney Kamlager-Dove — leaders proudly endorsed by the Jane Fonda Climate PAC — joined hundreds of protestors outside the Treasury Department to demand answers and reject Elon Musk’s hostile takeover of our government. We couldn’t be more proud of them. Musk has no business inside our federal payment systems. None. And yet, Donald Trump gave his handpicked team access to sensitive financial data that impacts all of us. Social Security payments. Medicare reimbursements. Tax refunds. Trillions of dollars in government transactions are now within reach of one of the most reckless and corrupt men on the planet. And what are most members of Congress doing about it? Too many are sitting idly by, hoping this all just blows over. Hoping that if they talk about “bipartisanship” enough, Trump and Musk will just... stop? No. That’s not how this works. **The only way out of this crisis is to fight like hell.** To demand that every member of Congress use every tool they have to shut this down. No more business as usual. No more waiting for Trump to “screw up.” We don’t have time for that. Yesterday’s protest showed us the kind of leadership we need — fierce, unapologetic, relentless. We need more of it. A lot more. We are all in — backing leaders who show up and refuse to let billionaires buy our democracy. But we need you in this fight, too. **[If you agree, chip in now to help us elect more fearless leaders who will fight back against Trump, Musk, and every corrupt actor trying to dismantle our government.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101611370/526506359/2105508926?refcode=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjlkNDZhNTI5LWZiZTMtZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=cAUto9eouJPfsLxOxMu5-TTn4LG-ZXAHj7HLxy7MQeo=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101611371/526506360/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjlkNDZhNTI5LWZiZTMtZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=cAUto9eouJPfsLxOxMu5-TTn4LG-ZXAHj7HLxy7MQeo=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101611372/526506361/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ms=ea.9d46a529-fbe3-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjlkNDZhNTI5LWZiZTMtZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=cAUto9eouJPfsLxOxMu5-TTn4LG-ZXAHj7HLxy7MQeo=)

clean_body: We need more of this from Democrats in Congress. | Yesterday, our endorsed leaders stepped up. Reps. Maxwell Frost, Jasmine Crockett, and Sydney Kamlager-Dove — leaders proudly endorsed by the Jane Fonda Climate PAC — joined hundreds of protestors outside the Treasury Department to demand answers and reject Elon Musk’s hostile takeover of our government. We couldn’t be more proud of them. Musk has no business inside our federal payment systems. None. And yet, Donald Trump gave his handpicked team access to sensitive financial data that impacts all of us. Social Security payments. Medicare reimbursements. Tax refunds. Trillions of dollars in government transactions are now within reach of one of the most reckless and corrupt men on the planet. And what are most members of Congress doing about it? Too many are sitting idly by, hoping this all just blows over. Hoping that if they talk about “bipartisanship” enough, Trump and Musk will just... stop? No. That’s not how this works. The only way out of this crisis is to fight like hell. To demand that every member of Congress use every tool they have to shut this down. No more business as usual. No more waiting for Trump to “screw up.” We don’t have time for that. Yesterday’s protest showed us the kind of leadership we need — fierce, unapologetic, relentless. We need more of it. A lot more. We are all in — backing leaders who show up and refuse to let billionaires buy our democracy. But we need you in this fight, too. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Let’s build a coalition worth fighting for. — Jane Fonda Climate PAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

We will never bend the knee to extreme MAGA Republicans who promote chaos, armed insurrection and releasing violent criminals who assault law enforcement. This is one more reason why we must retake the House majority. = = | | = = | = = | = = Keep the faith, Hakeem Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor. Now, as the top Democrat in the House, he’s fighting to stop MAGA extremism and reclaim the majority for Democrats. Hakeem is committed to electing a record number of Democrats in the next election and beyond. But he can’t compete with Republicans’ special interest cash without our support. Is there any way you could chip in to help? = We know you’re getting a lot of emails, but that’s because Democrats have a clear path to winning back the House – and Hakeem isn’t letting a single second go to waste. Peter,
---
The Democrats are fighting tooth and nail to keep Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and Tulsi Gabbard from being confirmed. Why? The answer is simple—they know those **two** patriots will pull back the curtain and shed some much-needed light on what’s been going on in the Democrats’ bloated bureaucracies. I am doing everything in my power to deliver President Trump the nominees he wants and the American people deserve. The people voted for this agenda. They voted for a healthy America—an America where the intelligence services target our enemies, not the public. It’s a critical mission, and their **confirmations** are a mission I gladly undertake. The future of America is at stake!
---

The Democrats are doing everything they can to block these strong leaders—leaders who will stand up to the deep state, expose corruption, and put America first. We cannot let them win.

I’m doing everything in my power to push back against the radical left’s agenda, but I need your help. **Will you chip in $25, $10, or even just $5 today to help me continue this fight and deliver the America-first leadership we voted for?** Every dollar counts in this critical battle! Fellow American,

**BREAKING:** Michigan's Democrat Senator Gary Peters just threw in the towel-he's NOT seeking reelection. **This means Michigan is officially IN PLAY for Republicans.**

Democrats are PANICKING. But make no mistake, they will spend whatever it takes, pull every dirty trick in the book, and unleash out-of-touch Hollywood elites and billionaires to keep this seat.

_" If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator **Phil Gramm** (R-TX)_
* * *
**AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED**
Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to [ATX Pulse](https://substack.com/redirect/3336c4ab-368f-4269-aaac-2a490298c92e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3bb94e2f-362c-441c-b9ab-c8ef882d7368?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here:_ [ATXpulsepremium.com](https://substack.com/redirect/d92b2946-ed2a-4775-8824-6e31f6dcb65d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Read today's FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/d723716c-c1ef-468f-9dff-e3503026cfda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/d92b2946-ed2a-4775-8824-6e31f6dcb65d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Senate to vote today on school choice bill,** " Dallas Morning News' **Philip Jankowski** -- "The Texas Senate is [poised to pass a "school choice" bill](https://substack.com/redirect/391cf846-f359-4bd9-bc93-909987dd7ac7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Wednesday that would allow parents to use taxpayer dollars for private school education.

Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick,** who presides over the Senate, has vowed to pass [Senate Bill 2](https://substack.com/redirect/5b7c3067-7f45-4170-8e25-f419cf0ea5a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and send it to the Texas House, where similar school voucher-style bills fell short in previous legislative sessions.

Gov. **Greg Abbott** has made school choice his [No. 1 policy goal](https://substack.com/redirect/c385dee0-41f3-4320-b74a-87cbd994ce06?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the Legislature.

The debate and votes will be livestreamed by the Texas Senate at [senate.texas.gov/av-live.php](https://substack.com/redirect/6b12c49d-cbe8-4915-95e9-1ad3b9f9aeff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The Senate session begins at noon, and SB 2 is the only bill set for debate Wednesday.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/cb9e734d-0710-4628-a1a3-0a48d8da7520?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
SB 2 would create a fund for education savings accounts, a school voucher-style program, with $1 billion from the state budget devoted to the fund. Officials have estimated it would provide enough funding for 90,000 students.

All Texas school-age children would be eligible to apply for an education savings account. Students chosen would get $10,000 to put toward a certified private school. Special education children could receive $11,500. Home-schooled children could get $2,000 to put toward their education.

The bill creates tiered access. If the number of children applying for an education savings account exceeds the $1 billion currently allocated for the fund, $800 million would be prioritized for low-income families or children receiving special education who previously attended public school.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/cb9e734d-0710-4628-a1a3-0a48d8da7520?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The bill defines low income as families earning five times the federal poverty level, or about $160,000 a year for a family of four.

The remaining $200 million would be available to children of all income levels and include those who are currently in private school.

Similar proposals have passed the Senate numerous times in previous years. Support for the bill falls largely on partisan lines in the Senate, where Republicans hold a strong majority of 20 out of 31 seats.

Patrick has boasted that SB 2 will face little resistance in the Senate, telling KTVT-TV last week that he would send the bill "like a rocket ship" to the House.

The House is a different story. In previous years, similar bills have been defeated by a coalition of Democrats and rural Republicans who opposed using taxpayer money for private school education.

Many House Republicans who opposed school choice retired or were unseated in bruising 2024 Republican primaries in which Abbott actively campaigned against incumbents. Abbott's political opposition was fueled by $10 million from GOP megadonor **Jeff Yass** , a Pennsylvania billionaire and leading school choice advocate.

Abbott has said he has at least 79 votes to pass the measure - a majority of the 150-member House." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/6f723809-259e-4964-8b45-c386220a4ce4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "Texas Senate to vote today on school choice bill, " Dallas Morning News' Philip Jankowski -- "The Texas Senate is Wednesday that would allow parents to use taxpayer dollars for private school education. Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick, who presides over the Senate, has vowed to pass and send it to the Texas House, where similar school voucher-style bills fell short in previous legislative sessions. Gov. Greg Abbott has made school choice his for the Legislature. The debate and votes will be livestreamed by the Texas Senate at . The Senate session begins at noon, and SB 2 is the only bill set for debate Wednesday. SB 2 would create a fund for education savings accounts, a school voucher-style program, with $1 billion from the state budget devoted to the fund. Officials have estimated it would provide enough funding for 90,000 students. All Texas school-age children would be eligible to apply for an education savings account. Students chosen would get $10,000 to put toward a certified private school. Special education children could receive $11,500. Home-schooled children could get $2,000 to put toward their education. The bill creates tiered access. If the number of children applying for an education savings account exceeds the $1 billion currently allocated for the fund, $800 million would be prioritized for low-income families or children receiving special education who previously attended public school. The bill defines low income as families earning five times the federal poverty level, or about $160,000 a year for a family of four. The remaining $200 million would be available to children of all income levels and include those who are currently in private school. Similar proposals have passed the Senate numerous times in previous years. Support for the bill falls largely on partisan lines in the Senate, where Republicans hold a strong majority of 20 out of 31 seats. Patrick has boasted that SB 2 will face little resistance in the Senate, telling KTVT-TV last week that he would send the bill "like a rocket ship" to the House. The House is a different story. In previous years, similar bills have been defeated by a coalition of Democrats and rural Republicans who opposed using taxpayer money for private school education. Many House Republicans who opposed school choice retired or were unseated in bruising 2024 Republican primaries in which Abbott actively campaigned against incumbents. Abbott's political opposition was fueled by $10 million from GOP megadonor Jeff Yass , a Pennsylvania billionaire and leading school choice advocate. Abbott has said he has at least 79 votes to pass the measure - a majority of the 150-member House." NY-21 is one of the most competitive races in the country right now. The GOP’s majority is incredibly small, and this special election will be one of our first chances to fight back.

| Hi Peter, it’s Blake Gendebien.

**I’m honored to be endorsed by the 15 Democratic County Chairs, making me the presumptive Democratic nominee in the potential special election for New York’s 21st Congressional District.**

[_**When Elise Stefanik resigns, a special election will be called, and we’ll have less than 90 days to campaign. Will you split a donation between me and Fight for Progress to become a Founding Donor of my campaign and help us prepare?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101613209/526512018/634970978?refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-02-05_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFjNGMwYjExLWZlZTMtZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=vnhyNWPDBZSKlfi8xTc2-7WbRPFeukNPa3Rq2rCkcGg=)

NY-21 is one of the most competitive races in the country right now. The GOP’s majority is incredibly small, and this special election will be one of our first chances to fight back.

**All eyes are on us to see if we have what it takes to win this race.**

As a dairy farmer, I’m used to rolling up my sleeves and working hard to get the job done. But flipping this seat won’t be easy, so I’m counting on your support to hit the ground running and connect with voters across the North Country.

name: Tamie Wilson
subject: Investigating the investigators? Give me a break
body:
alt [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/100067/images/LOGO.jpg]
Friends,
We told you Jim Jordan wasn’t going to use his power to help everyday people.
We told you he would waste taxpayer dollars chasing conspiracies instead of doing his actual job.
And now, here we are.
Last week, it was announced that Jim Jordan’s Judiciary Committee will oversee a new House panel aimed at undermining the bipartisan January 6th Committee— you know, the one that proved Donald Trump called for and incited an attack on our government.
You have to ask yourself: what exactly is Jordan hoping to find?
Evidence that Trump didn’t lead a violent mob to storm the Capitol?
That the hours of footage, sworn testimony, and criminal convictions were all just make-believe ?
Let’s be real—this isn’t about uncovering the truth.
This is about giving Jim Jordan exactly what he craves: a microphone, a camera, and endless opportunities to rant about fake scandals instead of helping the people of Ohio.
That’s why we’re fighting back. Our movement is gearing up to take this clown show head-on, but we need your help to do it. Will you chip in $25, $50, $100, $1,000, $2,500, or $3,300 today to help us hold Jim Jordan accountable? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tamie-wilson-c2g-post-elec?refcode=20250205_j6_investigation_link1]
How does a taxpayer-funded investigation of the investigators help working Ohioans?
Does it lower the cost of groceries?
Help people access healthcare?
Create jobs?
Of course not.
But it does keep Jordan on TV, pushing his dangerous lies and working overtime to protect Trump’s corruption.
He’s turned the Judiciary Committee into a political circus, wasting time and resources chasing “deep state” fantasies while ignoring the real problems families in Ohio are facing.
And now, he’s collaborating directly with Trump’s handpicked DOJ nominees to further dismantle any real oversight and accountability.
This is what the ULTRA-MAGA power grab looks like.
And we won’t let it go unanswered.
Today, I’ll be joining the National 50-State Anti-Trump Protest —and if you’re in Ohio, I hope you’ll stand with me at the Statehouse in Columbus at 12pm, and at Willard Park in Cleveland at 4 PM.
Let’s show Jim Jordan and this corrupt administration that we won’t be bullied into their authoritarian takeover of our government.
We’re building something big to take on Jordan and his ULTRA-MAGA cronies, and we’re just about ready to launch. Every dollar you contribute now will help us hit the ground running. We need you with us in this fight. Chip in today to help us fight back! Let’s Do This!! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tamie-wilson-c2g-post-elec?refcode=20250205_j6_investigation_link2]
Working for US,
Tamie Wilson

Donate to Tamie Today [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tamie-wilson-c2g-post-elec?refcode=20250205_j6_investigation_button]

Tamie Wilson For US
273 Saratoga St
Delaware, OH 43015
United States
www.tamiewilson.com [https://www.tamiewilson.com/]

Folks, Happy Black History Month! This month is dedicated to acknowledging the countless contributions that Black Americans have made to our state and nation throughout history. **But it’s also about recognizing the inequality that is still rooted in our society.** Voter suppression laws are purposefully designed to prevent Black and Brown folks from voting, and denying students the right to learn about the truth of America’s racist institutions is erasure of Black history. I will continue fighting for voting protections and accurate education in our schools this month and every month. Today and every day, let’s recommit ourselves to the fight for equal opportunity and justice. \- Jared
